Location, such as nose, right or left main should be referenced in the "Part Location" field unless the defective part is common to all locations. Typical parts are leveling cylinders, centering system, actuator brackets, bungees, emergency extension system parts, uplocks/downlocks, uplock/downlock actuator, drag braces, etc.
notes: Heuristic applicability; confirm against aircraft IPC/AMM/maintenance manual.
source: FAA Joint Aircraft System/Component Code Table and Definitions

title: Landing Gear Door Retract Section
definition: The nose and main landing gear door actuating system parts other than the actuator. Excludes door structure and hinges, which are to be filed in JASC code 5280. Typical parts are bellcrank, rod, sequence valve, latch, lines, hoses, etc.
notes: Heuristic applicability; confirm against aircraft IPC/AMM/maintenance manual.
source: FAA Joint Aircraft System/Component Code Table and Definitions

title: Landing Gear Door Actuator
definition: The actuating units that open and close the landing gear doors. Position on the aircraft (nose, left, or right main) should be shown in the "Part Location" field.
notes: Heuristic applicability; confirm against aircraft IPC/AMM/maintenance manual.
source: FAA Joint Aircraft System/Component Code Table and Definitions

title: Landing Gear Actuator
definition: The actuating units which retract and extend the nose or main gear. This includes electric motors, hydraulic cylinders but not self-contained electric motor driven hydraulic pumps such as power packs, which are filed in JASC code 2913. Specify “main gear” or “nose gear” in the Location data field.
notes: Heuristic applicability; confirm against aircraft IPC/AMM/maintenance manual.
source: FAA Joint Aircraft System/Component Code Table and Definitions

title: Landing Gear Selector
definition: The selector valves, switches, or control levers used to direct a power source to actuators for gear retraction and extension.
notes: Heuristic applicability; confirm against aircraft IPC/AMM/maintenance manual.
source: FAA Joint Aircraft System/Component Code Table and Definitions

title: Landing Gear Brake System
definition: The brake system miscellaneous parts other than the brake assembly, master cylinder, power valve and anti-skid system. Includes the pressure source and associated system for emergency brake actuation, and brake anti-ice system. Typical parts are line, hose, fitting, park brake valve, gauge, etc.
notes: Heuristic applicability; confirm against aircraft IPC/AMM/maintenance manual.
source: FAA Joint Aircraft System/Component Code Table and Definitions

title: Brake Anti-Skid Section
definition: The system units and parts that automatically control brake pressure during landing roll to prevent tire skidding. Typical parts are transducer, control box, valve, etc.
notes: Heuristic applicability; confirm against aircraft IPC/AMM/maintenance manual.
source: FAA Joint Aircraft System/Component Code Table and Definitions

title: Brake
definition: The parts of the brake unit mounted at the wheels only. The position on the aircraft should be shown in the "Part Location" field. Typical parts are disc, cylinder, lining, seal, rotor, housing, etc.
notes: Heuristic applicability; confirm against aircraft IPC/AMM/maintenance manual.
source: FAA Joint Aircraft System/Component Code Table and Definitions

title: Master Cylinder/Brake Valve
definition: The units that provide a power source for cylinder-power brake actuation. Does not include connecting lines to brake units, which are filed in JASC code 3240. Typical parts are seal, piston, housing, etc.
notes: Heuristic applicability; confirm against aircraft IPC/AMM/maintenance manual.
source: FAA Joint Aircraft System/Component Code Table and Definitions

title: Tire
definition: For reports of tire defects and failures. Include the manufacturer size and defect location in text. Identify the location of the tire in the "Part Location" field (i.e., nose, right main landing gear, "NLG", or "RT MLG").
notes: Heuristic applicability; confirm against aircraft IPC/AMM/maintenance manual.
source: FAA Joint Aircraft System/Component Code Table and Definitions

title: Tire Tube
definition: For reports of defective wheel tire tubes. Include the manufacturer name, tube type and size.
notes: Heuristic applicability; confirm against aircraft IPC/AMM/maintenance manual.
source: FAA Joint Aircraft System/Component Code Table and Definitions

title: Wheel/Ski/Float
definition: For reports of defective wheels, skis or seaplane floats and associated parts such as bearings, dust seals, bolts. The "Part Name" field should not refer to a part of the wheel which is defective such as "rim" or "half" that does not have separate part numbers. Such entries should be placed in the text. Identify the location of the wheel in the "Part Location" field (i.e., nose, right main landing gear, "NLG,"or "RT MLG"). The wheel, ski or float make, model, and part number should also be included in the report.
notes: Heuristic applicability; confirm against aircraft IPC/AMM/maintenance manual.
source: FAA Joint Aircraft System/Component Code Table and Definitions

title: Landing Gear Steering System
definition: The miscellaneous system parts other than the actuator, which provide for aircraft directional control on the ground. Includes main gear steering systems. Does not include wheel-braking systems. Typical parts are, cable, rod end, collar, line, valve, accumulator, etc.
notes: Heuristic applicability; confirm against aircraft IPC/AMM/maintenance manual.
source: FAA Joint Aircraft System/Component Code Table and Definitions

title: Steering Unit
definition: The actuator which turn the wheel(s) by a power source for controlling direction of movement on the ground. Typical parts are cylinder, seal, etc.
notes: Heuristic applicability; confirm against aircraft IPC/AMM/maintenance manual.
source: FAA Joint Aircraft System/Component Code Table and Definitions

title: Shimmy Damper
definition: The devices mounted on steerable wheel forks to reduce shimmy. Typical parts are seal, spring, housing, etc.
notes: Heuristic applicability; confirm against aircraft IPC/AMM/maintenance manual.
source: FAA Joint Aircraft System/Component Code Table and Definitions

title: Landing Gear Position and Warning
definition: The system parts which provide indication and warning of the landing gear position. Includes gear safety switches which prevent inadvertent actuation such as squat or air/ground sensor. Typical parts are relay, switch bracket, lamp, horn, uplock switch, downlock switch, in transit switch, etc.
notes: Heuristic applicability; confirm against aircraft IPC/AMM/maintenance manual.
source: FAA Joint Aircraft System/Component Code Table and Definitions

title: Auxiliary Gear (Tail Skid)
definition: The devices such as tail skids on tricycle gear aircraft used to stabilize the aircraft on the ground and to prevent ground contact damage. This code is also used for supplementary wheels on rotorcraft, skids for ground handling but not for skids or amphibian/seaplane floats, hull or associated retractable landing gear. This code is not for auxiliary or emergency landing gear extension systems which are filed in JASC code 3230.
notes: Heuristic applicability; confirm against aircraft IPC/AMM/maintenance manual.
source: FAA Joint Aircraft System/Component Code Table and Definitions

title: Landing Gear System Wiring
definition: For reports indicating a problem with wiring specific to the Landing Gear System.
notes: Heuristic applicability; confirm against aircraft IPC/AMM/maintenance manual.
source: FAA Joint Aircraft System/Component Code Table and Definitions

title: Lighting System
definition: The units and components which provide for external and internal illumination. Includes light fixtures, switches and wiring. Does not include warning lights for individual systems. Use this code for reports of a general nature or for miscellaneous external and internal lamps, circuitry, switches, etc., with insufficient information to file in a specific JASC 3300 series code. Warning lights are filed in the individual JASC Chapter 33 code.
notes: Heuristic applicability; confirm against aircraft IPC/AMM/maintenance manual.
source: FAA Joint Aircraft System/Component Code Table and Definitions

title: Flight Compartment Lighting
definition: The lighting systems and equipment including panel illumination other than inside individual instruments, master warning light systems such as annunciator panels, and associated dimming systems located in the flight compartment only. Typical parts are bulb, socket, switch, lamp, lens, relay, rheostat, resistor, ballast, etc.
notes: Heuristic applicability; confirm against aircraft IPC/AMM/maintenance manual.
source: FAA Joint Aircraft System/Component Code Table and Definitions

title: Passenger Compartment Lighting
definition: The lighting systems in the passenger seating compartment, lavatories, buffet/galley compartments and cabin carry-on baggage/coat areas. Includes lamps for illumination of cabin, reading lamps, seat belt/no-smoking signs and passenger call systems. Does not include emergency lighting which is to be filed in JASC code 3350. Typical parts are ballast, switch, transformer, lamp, etc.
notes: Heuristic applicability; confirm against aircraft IPC/AMM/maintenance manual.
source: FAA Joint Aircraft System/Component Code Table and Definitions

title: Cargo Compartment Lighting
definition: The lighting systems in the compartments used for storage of cargo, baggage, or aircraft system components which require servicing. Does not include electrical systems fire or smoke sensing. Typical parts are circuit breaker, lamp, lens, switch, etc.
notes: Heuristic applicability; confirm against aircraft IPC/AMM/maintenance manual.
source: FAA Joint Aircraft System/Component Code Table and Definitions

title: Exterior Lighting
definition: The lighting systems for illumination outside the aircraft such as landing, taxi, position, wing illumination including the rotating beacon and strobe. Typical parts are switch, lamp, power supply, lens, circuit breaker, flasher unit, relay, motor, wheel well lights, brackets, etc.
notes: Heuristic applicability; confirm against aircraft IPC/AMM/maintenance manual.
source: FAA Joint Aircraft System/Component Code Table and Definitions

title: Emergency Lighting
definition: The cabin, flight compartment, and exterior emergency lighting systems, which furnish illumination in event of electrical power failure. This includes batteries, lamps, and associated circuitry and parts for emergency exit lighting.
notes: Heuristic applicability; confirm against aircraft IPC/AMM/maintenance manual.
source: FAA Joint Aircraft System/Component Code Table and Definitions

title: Light System Wiring
definition: For reports indicating a problem with wiring specific to the Lighting Systems.
notes: Heuristic applicability; confirm against aircraft IPC/AMM/maintenance manual.
source: FAA Joint Aircraft System/Component Code Table and Definitions

title: Navigation System
definition: The units and components which provide aircraft navigational information. For reports which are of a general nature relating to the navigation systems. Also, for reports on equipment utilized in the flight inspection of airways systems (excluding avionics equipment normally used for flight operations of the aircraft). Use this code for reports with insufficient information to file in a more specific JASC 3400 series code. Typical flight inspection equipment would be computers, recorders, nav comms, guidance equipment, etc.
notes: Heuristic applicability; confirm against aircraft IPC/AMM/maintenance manual.
source: FAA Joint Aircraft System/Component Code Table and Definitions

title: Flight Environment Data
definition: The system which senses environmental conditions and uses the data to influence navigation.
notes: Heuristic applicability; confirm against aircraft IPC/AMM/maintenance manual.
source: FAA Joint Aircraft System/Component Code Table and Definitions

title: Pitot/Static System
definition: The system which provides a source of ram or static air for distribution to using instruments and pressure differential units such as automatic landing gear extender, altimeter, airspeed and rate of climb. Does not include the using units, instruments, the anti-ice heating elements, or the associated circuitry and switches which are filed in JASC code 3030. Typical parts are air pick up heads, lines, fittings, drain valves, static port, selector valve, etc.
notes: Heuristic applicability; confirm against aircraft IPC/AMM/maintenance manual.
source: FAA Joint Aircraft System/Component Code Table and Definitions

title: Outside Air Temperature Indicator/Sensor
definition: The unit mounted in the engine induction air intake to sense and transmit temperature to the cockpit indicator. Also for the sensors and instruments which measure and indicate the temperature of ambient air outside the aircraft. Includes associated circuitry and related parts. Typical parts are sensor, indicator, case, etc.
notes: Heuristic applicability; confirm against aircraft IPC/AMM/maintenance manual.
source: FAA Joint Aircraft System/Component Code Table and Definitions

title: Rate of Climb Indicator
definition: The instrument which senses and indicates the rate of climb or descent of an aircraft. Does not include the associated static system. Includes the instantaneous vertical speed indicator (IVSI).
notes: Heuristic applicability; confirm against aircraft IPC/AMM/maintenance manual.
source: FAA Joint Aircraft System/Component Code Table and Definitions

title: Airspeed/Mach Indicator
definition: The instrument which measures and indicates speed of the aircraft. Does not include the Doppler indicator which are filed in JASC code 3443.
notes: Heuristic applicability; confirm against aircraft IPC/AMM/maintenance manual.
source: FAA Joint Aircraft System/Component Code Table and Definitions

title: High Speed Warning
definition: The system components and parts, including the computer which sense, transmit and provide warning when operating air speed limits are exceeded. Typical parts are transducer, stall warning detector, switch, vane, horn, lamp, warning unit computer, module, etc.
notes: Heuristic applicability; confirm against aircraft IPC/AMM/maintenance manual.
source: FAA Joint Aircraft System/Component Code Table and Definitions

title: Altimeter, Barometric/Encoder
definition: Altimeters and barometric encoders used to measure and indicate altitude. Also includes the unit which senses and alerts to a change in a preselected altitude. Does not include the Ground Proximity Systems and radio/radar altimeters which are filed in JASC codes 3444. Typical parts are dial, case, pointer, spring, etc.
notes: Heuristic applicability; confirm against aircraft IPC/AMM/maintenance manual.
source: FAA Joint Aircraft System/Component Code Table and Definitions

title: Air Data Computer
definition: The computer and its integral parts which receives data from various environmental sensing systems, computes this data, and makes it available to the various navigation systems. Does not include external hardware such as cables, mounting racks, remote switches, etc., which are filed in JASC code 3410.
notes: Heuristic applicability; confirm against aircraft IPC/AMM/maintenance manual.
source: FAA Joint Aircraft System/Component Code Table and Definitions

title: Stall Warning System
definition: The system components and parts, including the computer which sense, transmit and provide aural, visual and stick shaker warning of an aircraft in an impending flight stall condition. Typical parts are transducer, stall warning detector, switch, vane, horn, lamp, stick shaker, heater element, warning unit computer, module, etc.
notes: Heuristic applicability; confirm against aircraft IPC/AMM/maintenance manual.
source: FAA Joint Aircraft System/Component Code Table and Definitions

title: Attitude and Direction Data System
definition: The system components and parts which use magnetic, gyroscopic, and inertia forces to indicate an aircraft attitude and direction. Use this code for reports with insufficient information to file in JASC codes 3421 through 3425. Includes such items as the inertial reference system (IRS), etc.
notes: Heuristic applicability; confirm against aircraft IPC/AMM/maintenance manual.
source: FAA Joint Aircraft System/Component Code Table and Definitions

title: Attitude Gyro and Indicating System
definition: The gyroscopic unit which supplies attitude information to the necessary systems; for instance, vertical reference outputs for use as roll and pitch data to the autopilot computer. Includes the instruments operating by the gyroscopic principle, driven by air flow or an electric motor. Typical parts are vertical gyro and the gyro horizon.
notes: Heuristic applicability; confirm against aircraft IPC/AMM/maintenance manual.
source: FAA Joint Aircraft System/Component Code Table and Definitions

title: Directional Gyro and Indicating System
definition: The unit operating by gyroscopic principle and driven by airflow or an electric motor, which provides heading (direction) references relative to a preset heading in degrees of the compass. Also for the flux unit detector which senses the earth's magnetic field and uses this data to correct for gyro drift. Typical parts are gyro, rotor, bearing, etc.
notes: Heuristic applicability; confirm against aircraft IPC/AMM/maintenance manual.
source: FAA Joint Aircraft System/Component Code Table and Definitions

title: Magnetic Compass
definition: The instrument which indicates the magnetic heading of an aircraft by self contained magnetized needles. Typical parts are compensator, adjusting screw, gasket, float, case, etc.
notes: Heuristic applicability; confirm against aircraft IPC/AMM/maintenance manual.
source: FAA Joint Aircraft System/Component Code Table and Definitions

title: Turn and Bank/Rate of Turn Indicator
definition: The instrument actuated by gyroscopic forces and driven by air flow or electric motor to indicate both rate of turn and angle of bank.
notes: Heuristic applicability; confirm against aircraft IPC/AMM/maintenance manual.
source: FAA Joint Aircraft System/Component Code Table and Definitions

title: Integrated Flight System
definition: The system which computes, interrogates, and continuously displays basic attitude, position, and steering information in order to maintain a particular course, heading, or attitude. Does not include flight management system components, which should be assigned to JASC code 3460. Typical parts are integrated flight annunciator, integrated flight comparator, integrated flight computer/amplifier, integrated flight control and integrated flight indicators (i.e., horizontal situation indicator (HSI), attitude and direction indicator (ADI), attitude direction unit (ADU), heading and direction indicator (HDI), radio direction indicator (RDI), course direction indicator (CDI), flight director indicator (FDI), pictorial navigation indicator, flight command indicator, steering computer utilized in the integrated flight instrument systems, and other components such as cables, connectors, etc.
notes: Heuristic applicability; confirm against aircraft IPC/AMM/maintenance manual.
source: FAA Joint Aircraft System/Component Code Table and Definitions

title: Landing and Taxi Aids
definition: The system providing guidance during approach, landing and taxiing. Includes such items as, ILS, paravisual director, ground guidance systems, markers, etc.
notes: Heuristic applicability; confirm against aircraft IPC/AMM/maintenance manual.
source: FAA Joint Aircraft System/Component Code Table and Definitions

title: Localizer/VOR System
definition: The electronic portion of an instrument landing system (ILS) that indicates the centerline of the runway to the pilot. For reports on localizer/very high frequency omni range (VOR) systems. Typical parts are receiver, antenna, indicator, circuit breaker, switch, antenna coax, etc.
notes: Heuristic applicability; confirm against aircraft IPC/AMM/maintenance manual.
source: FAA Joint Aircraft System/Component Code Table and Definitions

title: Glide Slope System
definition: The system which provides an instrument needle reference from an electronic signal radiated from a ground transmitter to enable the pilot to fly the proper glide path for landing under instrument meteorological conditions. Typical parts are circuit breaker, switch, receiver, antenna, indicator, etc.
notes: Heuristic applicability; confirm against aircraft IPC/AMM/maintenance manual.
source: FAA Joint Aircraft System/Component Code Table and Definitions

title: Microwave Landing System
definition: The instrumental landing system operating in the microwave spectrum which provides lateral and vertical guidance to aircraft having compatible avionics equipment. Typical parts are receiver, antenna, control panel, etc.
notes: Heuristic applicability; confirm against aircraft IPC/AMM/maintenance manual.
source: FAA Joint Aircraft System/Component Code Table and Definitions

title: Marker Beacon System
definition: The system which provides an aural and visual indication of passage over specified points on the glide path for landing under instrument meteorological conditions. NOTE: In instances where the control panel is an integral portion of the audio control panel, it would be filed in JASC code 2350. Typical parts are marker beacon antenna, receivers, visual/aural indication units, marker light, control panel, etc.
notes: Heuristic applicability; confirm against aircraft IPC/AMM/maintenance manual.
source: FAA Joint Aircraft System/Component Code Table and Definitions

title: Heads Up Display System
definition: The flight instrument system that allows the pilot of an aircraft to watch the flight instruments while looking ahead of the aircraft. Includes the display screen which allows information to be visually presented to the pilot while looking through the windscreen or at the control panel.
notes: Heuristic applicability; confirm against aircraft IPC/AMM/maintenance manual.
source: FAA Joint Aircraft System/Component Code Table and Definitions

title: Wind Shear Detection System
definition: The flight instrument system that allows the pilot to detect strong horizontal or vertical wind shift that acts at right angles to the direction the wind is blowing. Includes the outboard sensors, indicators, and the warning system which notifies the pilot of the appropriate corrective action maneuver to take.
notes: Heuristic applicability; confirm against aircraft IPC/AMM/maintenance manual.
source: FAA Joint Aircraft System/Component Code Table and Definitions

title: Independent Position Determining System
definition: The system which provides information to determine position and is primarily independent of ground installations. Use this code for reports of a general nature or for reports containing insufficient information to file in a more specific code identified in JASC codes 3441 through 3446. Typical parts are star tracker, sextants/octants, etc.
notes: Heuristic applicability; confirm against aircraft IPC/AMM/maintenance manual.
source: FAA Joint Aircraft System/Component Code Table and Definitions

title: Inertial Guidance System
definition: The navigation system which relies upon gyro platforms and accelerometers for its operation. Includes the control panel for the inertial navigation system; the instruments which receives their signal from the Inertial Navigation Unit (INU); and the unit containing the inertial platform and digital computer portion of the system. Use this JASC code for hardware components which do not have specific JASC codes assigned to them or when a system malfunction or failure occurs but the exact cause is not known. Typical parts are mode selector unit (MSU), control display unit (CDU), remote display unit (RDU), etc.
notes: Heuristic applicability; confirm against aircraft IPC/AMM/maintenance manual.
source: FAA Joint Aircraft System/Component Code Table and Definitions

title: Weather Radar System
definition: The system components and parts which transmits and receives a signal independent of ground facilities to determine the relative position of adverse weather cells. Typical parts are transceiver, antenna, control panel for the weather avoidance radar system, accessory synchronizers, servo amplifier, scope, etc.
notes: Heuristic applicability; confirm against aircraft IPC/AMM/maintenance manual.
source: FAA Joint Aircraft System/Component Code Table and Definitions

title: Doppler System
definition: The airborne radar system which utilizes the Doppler effect to measure and display ground speed, drift angle, cross track, etc.
notes: Heuristic applicability; confirm against aircraft IPC/AMM/maintenance manual.
source: FAA Joint Aircraft System/Component Code Table and Definitions

title: Ground Proximity System
definition: The system which detects and alerts flight crew to potential terrain hazards. Includes the antenna which transmits and receives an electronic signal for the radio altimeter equipment used for terrain-to-aircraft distance. Also includes the component which interprets a radio signal reflected back to a receiver to determine distance from the nearest terrain; and the component which process the warning computer input signals from various sources in order to determine if and when the crew should be alerted of a terrain hazard.
notes: Heuristic applicability; confirm against aircraft IPC/AMM/maintenance manual.
source: FAA Joint Aircraft System/Component Code Table and Definitions

title: Air Collision Avoidance System
definition: The system which provides information to determine aircraft position and is primarily independent of ground installations (i.e., traffic alert and collision avoidance system - TCAS). Use this code only if the specific system creating the problem cannot be established. Typical parts are collision avoidance monitoring units, etc.
notes: Heuristic applicability; confirm against aircraft IPC/AMM/maintenance manual.
source: FAA Joint Aircraft System/Component Code Table and Definitions

title: Non Radar Weather System
definition: The non radar weather system and components which sense the electrostatic charges accumulated around a storm cell in order to "map out" that cell on an indicator.
notes: Heuristic applicability; confirm against aircraft IPC/AMM/maintenance manual.
source: FAA Joint Aircraft System/Component Code Table and Definitions

title: Dependent Position Determining System
definition: The system which provides information to determine position and is mainly dependent on ground installations. Use this code for reports of a general nature or for those with insufficient information to file in a more specific JASC code identified in JASC codes 3451 through 3457.
notes: Heuristic applicability; confirm against aircraft IPC/AMM/maintenance manual.
source: FAA Joint Aircraft System/Component Code Table and Definitions

title: DME/TACAN System
definition: The systems which measures time-to-station, ground speed, and distance to a known transmitter location by transmitting and receiving electronic pulse signals (i.e., distance measuring equipment - DME; ultra high frequency tactical air navigational aid - TACAN). Typical parts are antenna, control unit, transceiver, coaxial cables, etc.
notes: Heuristic applicability; confirm against aircraft IPC/AMM/maintenance manual.
source: FAA Joint Aircraft System/Component Code Table and Definitions

title: ATC Transponder System
definition: The air traffic control (ATC) system which receives coded signals from a ground station and transmits a coded reply for altitude reporting and identification purposes. Typical parts are transponder, antenna, control unit, transceiver, coaxial connecting cables, etc.
notes: Heuristic applicability; confirm against aircraft IPC/AMM/maintenance manual.
source: FAA Joint Aircraft System/Component Code Table and Definitions

title: Long-Range Navigation (LORAN) System
definition: The radio navigation system and associated components and parts which provides for long range navigation (LORAN) enroute when operating on signals from ground based master and slave transmitting stations. Typical parts are antenna, coupler, CPU, receiver, indicator, etc.
notes: Heuristic applicability; confirm against aircraft IPC/AMM/maintenance manual.
source: FAA Joint Aircraft System/Component Code Table and Definitions

title: Visual Omnirange (VOR) System
definition: The radio navigation system in the very high frequency (VHF) band used for determining position relative to a ground transmitter and permits selection of an infinite number of magnetic courses for navigation to a transmitter (i.e., visual omnirange - VOR system). Typical parts are receiver, antenna, control panel, etc.
notes: Heuristic applicability; confirm against aircraft IPC/AMM/maintenance manual.
source: FAA Joint Aircraft System/Component Code Table and Definitions

title: Automatic Direction Finder (ADF) System
definition: The low frequency band system which receives a signal from a non-directional radio beacon to determine relative position from the beacon location (i.e., automatic direction finder - ADF system). Typical parts are antenna, control unit, receiver, coaxial cables, etc.
notes: Heuristic applicability; confirm against aircraft IPC/AMM/maintenance manual.
source: FAA Joint Aircraft System/Component Code Table and Definitions

title: Omega Navigation System
definition: The low frequency navigation system which provides for system geographical location of the aircraft on a worldwide basis when operating on signals from ground-based OMEGA and VHF transmitting stations. Typical parts are antenna, control unit or receiver, coaxial connecting cable, remote switches, connectors, etc.
notes: Heuristic applicability; confirm against aircraft IPC/AMM/maintenance manual.
source: FAA Joint Aircraft System/Component Code Table and Definitions

title: Global Positioning System
definition: The systems which are mainly dependent upon signals from ground transmitters or orbital satellites for their operations; systems such as VOR, ADF, DME, etc. Use this JASC code when there is insufficient information to assign one of the specific using system codes. Typical parts are antenna, control unit or receiver, coaxial connecting cable, remote switches, connectors, etc.
notes: Heuristic applicability; confirm against aircraft IPC/AMM/maintenance manual.
source: FAA Joint Aircraft System/Component Code Table and Definitions

title: Flight Management Computing Hardware System
definition: The hardware systems which combines navigational data to compute or manage the aircraft's geographical position or theoretical flight path. Typical parts are course computers, flight management computers, performance data computers, and associated control display units, warning annunciators, etc.
notes: Heuristic applicability; confirm against aircraft IPC/AMM/maintenance manual.
source: FAA Joint Aircraft System/Component Code Table and Definitions

title: Flight Management Computing Software System
definition: The software system which combines navigational data to compute or manage the aircraft's geographical position or theoretical flight path.
notes: Heuristic applicability; confirm against aircraft IPC/AMM/maintenance manual.
source: FAA Joint Aircraft System/Component Code Table and Definitions

title: Navigation System Wiring
definition: For reports indicating a problem with wiring specific to the Navigation Systems.
notes: Heuristic applicability; confirm against aircraft IPC/AMM/maintenance manual.
source: FAA Joint Aircraft System/Component Code Table and Definitions

title: Oxygen System
definition: The units and components which store, regulate, and deliver breathing oxygen to the passengers and crew. Typical parts are bottles, relief valves, shut-off valves, outlets, regulators, masks, walk-around bottles, etc.
notes: Heuristic applicability; confirm against aircraft IPC/AMM/maintenance manual.
source: FAA Joint Aircraft System/Component Code Table and Definitions

title: Crew Oxygen System
definition: The portion of the main system which furnishes oxygen to the crew.
notes: Heuristic applicability; confirm against aircraft IPC/AMM/maintenance manual.
source: FAA Joint Aircraft System/Component Code Table and Definitions

title: Passenger Oxygen System
definition: The portion of the main system which furnishes oxygen to the passengers.
notes: Heuristic applicability; confirm against aircraft IPC/AMM/maintenance manual.
source: FAA Joint Aircraft System/Component Code Table and Definitions

title: Portable Oxygen System
definition: The equipment attached to the portable bottle to regulate and dispense breathing oxygen, including the storage bottle for the portable oxygen system.
notes: Heuristic applicability; confirm against aircraft IPC/AMM/maintenance manual.
source: FAA Joint Aircraft System/Component Code Table and Definitions

title: Oxygen System Wiring
definition: For reports indicating a problem with wiring specific to the Oxygen System.
notes: Heuristic applicability; confirm against aircraft IPC/AMM/maintenance manual.
source: FAA Joint Aircraft System/Component Code Table and Definitions

title: Pneumatic System
definition: The units and components which deliver large volumes of compressed air from a power source to connecting points for other systems such as air conditioning, pressurization, deicing, etc.
notes: Heuristic applicability; confirm against aircraft IPC/AMM/maintenance manual.
source: FAA Joint Aircraft System/Component Code Table and Definitions

title: Pneumatic Distribution System
definition: Components and parts other than the regulator and shutoff valves delivering large volumes of compressed air from a power source to the control valves of using systems such as conditioning, pressurization. Doesn't include engine and airfoil anti-icing/deicing systems. Typical parts are regulator valve, actuator, duct, ducts valves, manifold, clamp, flow venturi, bellows, wye duct, check valve.
notes: Heuristic applicability; confirm against aircraft IPC/AMM/maintenance manual.
source: FAA Joint Aircraft System/Component Code Table and Definitions

title: Pneumatic Indicating System
definition: The system components and parts which sense, transmit, and indicate the temperature and pressure of air in the distribution system other than the pressure indicator or sensor. Includes the instrument which indicates air pressure in the pneumatic distributing systems. Does not include the using systems.
notes: Heuristic applicability; confirm against aircraft IPC/AMM/maintenance manual.
source: FAA Joint Aircraft System/Component Code Table and Definitions

title: Pneumatic System Wiring
definition: For reports indicating a problem with wiring specific to the Pneumatic System.
notes: Heuristic applicability; confirm against aircraft IPC/AMM/maintenance manual.
source: FAA Joint Aircraft System/Component Code Table and Definitions

title: Vacuum System
definition: The units and components used to generate, deliver, and regulate negative air pressure. Use this code for general reports of the pressure/vacuum system with insufficient information to file in a more specific JASC 3700 series code.
notes: Heuristic applicability; confirm against aircraft IPC/AMM/maintenance manual.
source: FAA Joint Aircraft System/Component Code Table and Definitions

title: Vacuum Distribution System
definition: The system components and parts, including the pump, regulator, oil separator or indication system, which are used to distribute low volume, negative pressure air (suction) to systems such as gyroscopic flight instruments, cabin rate controller, etc.; and to distribute low volume, positive pressure air to systems such as air foil deicer boots. Does not include the using systems. Typical parts are pump, filter, regulator, lines, manifold, check valves, element, etc.
notes: Heuristic applicability; confirm against aircraft IPC/AMM/maintenance manual.
source: FAA Joint Aircraft System/Component Code Table and Definitions

title: Vacuum Indicating System
definition: The system components and parts including which indicates negative air pressure in the vacuum lines. Includes the indicator and warning systems. Typical parts are the vacuum indicator and associated lines.
notes: Heuristic applicability; confirm against aircraft IPC/AMM/maintenance manual.
source: FAA Joint Aircraft System/Component Code Table and Definitions

title: Vacuum System Wiring
definition: For reports indicating a problem with wiring specific to the Vacuum System.
notes: Heuristic applicability; confirm against aircraft IPC/AMM/maintenance manual.
source: FAA Joint Aircraft System/Component Code Table and Definitions

title: Water and Waste System
definition: The fixed units and components which store and deliver fresh water. Also includes those fixed components which store and furnish a means of removal of water and waste. Use this code for reports with insufficient information to file in a more specific JASC 3800 series code. This code is also used for reports common to two or more systems.
notes: Heuristic applicability; confirm against aircraft IPC/AMM/maintenance manual.
source: FAA Joint Aircraft System/Component Code Table and Definitions

title: Potable Water System
definition: The system which is used to store and deliver fresh drinking water.
notes: Heuristic applicability; confirm against aircraft IPC/AMM/maintenance manual.
source: FAA Joint Aircraft System/Component Code Table and Definitions

title: Wash Water System
definition: The system which is used to store and deliver wash water.
notes: Heuristic applicability; confirm against aircraft IPC/AMM/maintenance manual.
source: FAA Joint Aircraft System/Component Code Table and Definitions

title: Waste Disposal System
definition: The system and components used for the disposal of water and waste. Includes wash basins, water closets, flush systems and collection tanks. Typical parts are valve, flush motor, lines, timer, etc.
notes: Heuristic applicability; confirm against aircraft IPC/AMM/maintenance manual.
source: FAA Joint Aircraft System/Component Code Table and Definitions

title: Air Supply (Water Pressure System)
definition: The system which provides the pressure to distribute potable water to the lavatories, etc. Typical parts are pump, motor, lines, etc.
notes: Heuristic applicability; confirm against aircraft IPC/AMM/maintenance manual.
source: FAA Joint Aircraft System/Component Code Table and Definitions

title: Water/Waste System Wiring
definition: For reports indicating a problem with wiring specific to the Water/Waste System.
notes: Heuristic applicability; confirm against aircraft IPC/AMM/maintenance manual.
source: FAA Joint Aircraft System/Component Code Table and Definitions

title: Central Maintenance Computer
definition: The unit, components and associated systems which interface with other airplane systems and provides a convenient way of communicating system problems to aircraft maintenance personnel. The system contains checkout and fault isolation procedures using a central computer to locate a single system or component malfunction. Typical parts are computer, storage devices, controls, display, etc.
notes: Heuristic applicability; confirm against aircraft IPC/AMM/maintenance manual.
source: FAA Joint Aircraft System/Component Code Table and Definitions

title: Central Maintenance System Wiring
definition: For reports indicating a problem with wiring specific to the Central Maintenance System.
notes: Heuristic applicability; confirm against aircraft IPC/AMM/maintenance manual.
source: FAA Joint Aircraft System/Component Code Table and Definitions

title: Airborne Auxiliary Power Unit (APU) System
definition: The airborne auxiliary power units (APU) installed on aircraft for the purpose of generating and supplying a single type or combination of auxiliary electric, hydraulic, pneumatic or other power. Does not include generators, alternators, hydraulic pumps, etc., or their connecting systems which supply and deliver power to their respective aircraft systems. Use this code for reports of a general nature with insufficient information to file in a specific JASC 4900 series code, or for reports which involve two or more APU systems. An entry "APU" is acceptable if no specific part is reported. The APU as well as aircraft make and model should be reported if known.
notes: Heuristic applicability; confirm against aircraft IPC/AMM/maintenance manual.
source: FAA Joint Aircraft System/Component Code Table and Definitions

title: APU Cowling/Containment
definition: The system of cowling and other components used to cover the auxiliary power unit, and contain any broken parts in the event of an external failure.
notes: Heuristic applicability; confirm against aircraft IPC/AMM/maintenance manual.
source: FAA Joint Aircraft System/Component Code Table and Definitions

title: APU Core Engine
definition: For reports of basic engine defects such as compressor, turbine, cases other than specific systems shown in other APU sub-systems such as fuel, ignition, exhaust, starting and controls. The APU make and model should be included if available. Typical parts are turbine, bearing, seal, impeller, blade, case, burner can, etc.
notes: Heuristic applicability; confirm against aircraft IPC/AMM/maintenance manual.
source: FAA Joint Aircraft System/Component Code Table and Definitions

title: APU Engine Fuel and Control
definition: The system and components which furnishes fuel from the aircraft tanks to the APU fuel control and associated injector nozzles. Including the unit which provides fuel at the proper pressure for fuel control operation; and the unit controlling and injecting metered fuel to the engine burner can section. Typical parts are shutoff valve, line, fitting, etc.
notes: Heuristic applicability; confirm against aircraft IPC/AMM/maintenance manual.
source: FAA Joint Aircraft System/Component Code Table and Definitions

title: APU Start/Ignition System
definition: The system units used to start the APU engine. Including the unit which provides a power source to the igniter during the starting cycle. Typical parts are ignition unit, magneto, igniter, starter, etc.
notes: Heuristic applicability; confirm against aircraft IPC/AMM/maintenance manual.
source: FAA Joint Aircraft System/Component Code Table and Definitions

title: APU Bleed Air System
definition: The system and components which provide and control a source of pressure and high volume of air for aircraft using systems such as engine starting, cabin air conditioning prior to starting engines. Typical parts are duct, bleed valve, clamp, seal, etc.
notes: Heuristic applicability; confirm against aircraft IPC/AMM/maintenance manual.
source: FAA Joint Aircraft System/Component Code Table and Definitions

title: APU Controls
definition: The system components which electrically and manually control operation of the APU engine. Typical parts are relay, control box, etc.
notes: Heuristic applicability; confirm against aircraft IPC/AMM/maintenance manual.
source: FAA Joint Aircraft System/Component Code Table and Definitions

title: APU Indicating System
definition: For general reports of APU operation indicating including the temperature indicator, tachometer generator or indicator (engine speed). Includes the instrument and associated warning system which sense, transmits, and indicates APU engine speed and temperatures to the flight crew.
notes: Heuristic applicability; confirm against aircraft IPC/AMM/maintenance manual.
source: FAA Joint Aircraft System/Component Code Table and Definitions

title: APU Exhaust System
definition: The components and parts which collect and direct exhaust gasses from the APU turbine to the aircraft exterior. Includes the movable door fairing. Typical parts are nozzle, door, actuator, seal, clamp, and shield.
notes: Heuristic applicability; confirm against aircraft IPC/AMM/maintenance manual.
source: FAA Joint Aircraft System/Component Code Table and Definitions

title: APU Oil System
definition: The system and components used for APU engine lubrication. Typical parts are filter, pump, relief valve, hose, line, etc.
notes: Heuristic applicability; confirm against aircraft IPC/AMM/maintenance manual.
source: FAA Joint Aircraft System/Component Code Table and Definitions

title: APU System Wiring
definition: For reports indicating a problem with wiring specific to the APU System.
notes: Heuristic applicability; confirm against aircraft IPC/AMM/maintenance manual.
source: FAA Joint Aircraft System/Component Code Table and Definitions

title: Standard Practices/Structures
definition: The standard practices and general procedures for typical repairs applicable to more than one chapter that are not specifically covered under JASC Chapters 52 through 57.
notes: Heuristic applicability; confirm against aircraft IPC/AMM/maintenance manual.
source: FAA Joint Aircraft System/Component Code Table and Definitions

title: Aircraft Structures
definition: For reports of aircraft structural problems of a general nature which affects two or more areas. Includes reports of lightning strikes which cannot be associated with a specific JASC code.
notes: Heuristic applicability; confirm against aircraft IPC/AMM/maintenance manual.
source: FAA Joint Aircraft System/Component Code Table and Definitions

title: Balloon Reports
definition: For all balloon reports irrespective of location or component involved. Includes all airship reports irrespective of location or component involved. Also includes reports for water ballast. Typical parts are stitching, seam, fabric, burner, valve, handle, valve seat, manifold and burner fuel tank, etc.
notes: Heuristic applicability; confirm against aircraft IPC/AMM/maintenance manual.
source: FAA Joint Aircraft System/Component Code Table and Definitions

title: Doors
definition: The removable units used for entrance or exit, and for enclosing other structure contained within the fuselage. Includes passenger and crew doors, cargo doors, emergency exits, etc. Electrical and hydraulic systems associated with door control are included as appropriate. Use this code for door reports of a general nature which affect two or more specific type of doors or are reported with insufficient information to file in a more specific JASC 5200 series code.
notes: Heuristic applicability; confirm against aircraft IPC/AMM/maintenance manual.
source: FAA Joint Aircraft System/Component Code Table and Definitions

title: Passenger/Crew Doors
definition: For reports of cabin entrance doors. Does not include door frames, warning systems, or cabin emergency exit doors/hatches. Typical parts are hinges, actuators, latches, handle, seals, structure, spring, cable, bellcrank, skin, etc.
notes: Heuristic applicability; confirm against aircraft IPC/AMM/maintenance manual.
source: FAA Joint Aircraft System/Component Code Table and Definitions

title: Emergency Exits
definition: For reports of emergency exit doors, windows and hatches. Typical parts are pan, hinge, latch, hook, etc.
notes: Heuristic applicability; confirm against aircraft IPC/AMM/maintenance manual.
source: FAA Joint Aircraft System/Component Code Table and Definitions

title: Cargo/Baggage Doors
definition: For exterior doors used to gain access to cargo or baggage storage areas. Does not include door frames on fuselage, door warning or compartment interior furnishings. Typical parts are door structure, seal, hinge, latch, latch pin, handle, skin, etc.
notes: Heuristic applicability; confirm against aircraft IPC/AMM/maintenance manual.
source: FAA Joint Aircraft System/Component Code Table and Definitions

title: Service Doors
definition: For reports pertaining to exterior doors used to gain access for servicing aircraft systems and equipment. Does not include the fluid service doors which are covered in JASC 5246.
notes: Heuristic applicability; confirm against aircraft IPC/AMM/maintenance manual.
source: FAA Joint Aircraft System/Component Code Table and Definitions

title: Galley Doors
definition: For reports pertaining to the galley door. Typical parts are hinges, structure, and the latch mechanism.
notes: Heuristic applicability; confirm against aircraft IPC/AMM/maintenance manual.
source: FAA Joint Aircraft System/Component Code Table and Definitions

title: Electrical/Electronic Compartment Doors
definition: For reports pertaining to the electrical/electronic compartment doors. Typical parts are hinges, structure, and the latch mechanism.
notes: Heuristic applicability; confirm against aircraft IPC/AMM/maintenance manual.
source: FAA Joint Aircraft System/Component Code Table and Definitions

title: Hydraulic Compartment Doors
definition: For reports pertaining to the hydraulic compartment doors. Typical parts are hinges, structure, and the latch mechanism.
notes: Heuristic applicability; confirm against aircraft IPC/AMM/maintenance manual.
source: FAA Joint Aircraft System/Component Code Table and Definitions

title: Accessory Compartment Doors
definition: For reports pertaining to the accessory compartment doors. Typical parts are hinges, structure, and the latch mechanism.
notes: Heuristic applicability; confirm against aircraft IPC/AMM/maintenance manual.
source: FAA Joint Aircraft System/Component Code Table and Definitions

title: Air Conditioning Doors
definition: For reports pertaining to doors used to gain access to the air conditioning compartment system and components. Typical parts are hinges, structure, and the latch mechanism.
notes: Heuristic applicability; confirm against aircraft IPC/AMM/maintenance manual.
source: FAA Joint Aircraft System/Component Code Table and Definitions

title: Fluid Service Doors
definition: For reports of service doors used to gain access to fluid service areas, excluding compartment doors which are filed in JASC code 5243. Typical parts are hinges, structure, and the latch mechanism.
notes: Heuristic applicability; confirm against aircraft IPC/AMM/maintenance manual.
source: FAA Joint Aircraft System/Component Code Table and Definitions

title: Auxiliary Power Unit (APU) Doors
definition: For reports of doors used to gain access for servicing the APU and components. Typical parts are hinges, structure, and the latch mechanism.
notes: Heuristic applicability; confirm against aircraft IPC/AMM/maintenance manual.
source: FAA Joint Aircraft System/Component Code Table and Definitions

title: Tail Cone Doors
definition: For reports pertaining to the tail cone door. Typical parts are hinges, structure, and the latch mechanism.
notes: Heuristic applicability; confirm against aircraft IPC/AMM/maintenance manual.
source: FAA Joint Aircraft System/Component Code Table and Definitions

title: Fixed Inner Doors
definition: For reports of doors within the fuselage in fixed partitions. Typical parts are structure, hinges, latches, lining but does not include doors in movable partitions.
notes: Heuristic applicability; confirm against aircraft IPC/AMM/maintenance manual.
source: FAA Joint Aircraft System/Component Code Table and Definitions

title: Entrance Stairs
definition: For reports of cabin entrance stairs which operate in conjunction with but are not an integral part of entrance doors. Typical parts are structure, actuator, controls and handrails, step, cable, bungee, latch hook, latch, bracket, bellcrank, etc.
notes: Heuristic applicability; confirm against aircraft IPC/AMM/maintenance manual.
source: FAA Joint Aircraft System/Component Code Table and Definitions

title: Door Warning System
definition: The system which is used to indicate to flight crews whether the exterior doors are closed and properly latched. Does not include the landing gear position warning indications which are covered in JASC code 3260. Typical parts are switch, lamp, horn, relay, etc. The suspect door should be identified if known.
notes: Heuristic applicability; confirm against aircraft IPC/AMM/maintenance manual.
source: FAA Joint Aircraft System/Component Code Table and Definitions

title: Landing Gear Doors
definition: For reports pertaining to the structural aspects of landing gear doors including hinges and seals on the wing, landing gear, and fuselage mounted doors. The door position on the aircraft or landing gear (i.e., nose, right main outboard, etc.) should be shown in the part location field. Does not include the operating mechanism or position indicating or warning system which is filed in JASC codes 3231 or 3260.
notes: Heuristic applicability; confirm against aircraft IPC/AMM/maintenance manual.
source: FAA Joint Aircraft System/Component Code Table and Definitions

title: Door System Wiring
definition: For reports indicating a problem with wiring specific to the Door Systems.
notes: Heuristic applicability; confirm against aircraft IPC/AMM/maintenance manual.
source: FAA Joint Aircraft System/Component Code Table and Definitions

title: Fuselage Structure (General)
definition: For reports of structural units and associated components and members which make up the compartments for crew, passengers, equipment, cargo, plus the structure of the envelope and gondola of airships.
notes: Heuristic applicability; confirm against aircraft IPC/AMM/maintenance manual.
source: FAA Joint Aircraft System/Component Code Table and Definitions

title: Aerial Tow Equipment
definition: For reports of defective aerial tow equipment including the attachments on fuselage and release mechanism.
notes: Heuristic applicability; confirm against aircraft IPC/AMM/maintenance manual.
source: FAA Joint Aircraft System/Component Code Table and Definitions

title: Rotorcraft Tail Boom
definition: For reports of the structure including exterior skin and truss framework of tail booms on rotorcraft. Includes attach fittings, etc., for tail boom and stabilizer surfaces. Typical parts are bulkhead, bracket, frame, frame tube, plate, etc.
notes: Heuristic applicability; confirm against aircraft IPC/AMM/maintenance manual.
source: FAA Joint Aircraft System/Component Code Table and Definitions

title: Fuselage Main, Structure
definition: For general reports of fuselage structure defects which affect two or more related parts or are reported with insufficient information to file in a more specific JASC 5300 series code. Use of this code should be avoided if possible.
notes: Heuristic applicability; confirm against aircraft IPC/AMM/maintenance manual.
source: FAA Joint Aircraft System/Component Code Table and Definitions

title: Fuselage Main, Frame
definition: For reports of the main fuselage frames. The associated attach fittings are covered in JASC code 5320.
notes: Heuristic applicability; confirm against aircraft IPC/AMM/maintenance manual.
source: FAA Joint Aircraft System/Component Code Table and Definitions

title: Fuselage Main, Bulkhead
definition: For reports of the main fuselage bulkheads and the associated attach fittings.
notes: Heuristic applicability; confirm against aircraft IPC/AMM/maintenance manual.
source: FAA Joint Aircraft System/Component Code Table and Definitions

title: Fuselage Main, Longeron/Stringer
definition: For reports of the main fuselage longerons/stringers.
notes: Heuristic applicability; confirm against aircraft IPC/AMM/maintenance manual.
source: FAA Joint Aircraft System/Component Code Table and Definitions

title: Fuselage Main, Keel
definition: For reports of the main fuselage keel beams.
notes: Heuristic applicability; confirm against aircraft IPC/AMM/maintenance manual.
source: FAA Joint Aircraft System/Component Code Table and Definitions

title: Fuselage Main, Floor Beam
definition: For reports of the main fuselage floor beams.
notes: Heuristic applicability; confirm against aircraft IPC/AMM/maintenance manual.
source: FAA Joint Aircraft System/Component Code Table and Definitions

title: Fuselage Miscellaneous Structure
definition: For reports of miscellaneous structure on the main fuselage structure which aids in the support of the primary structure. Includes such items as brackets, channels, stiffeners, clips, doublers, etc. Does not include movable partitions which are covered in JASC Chapter 25.
notes: Heuristic applicability; confirm against aircraft IPC/AMM/maintenance manual.
source: FAA Joint Aircraft System/Component Code Table and Definitions

title: Fuselage Floor Panel
definition: For reports of the interior floor panels within the main fuselage structure.
notes: Heuristic applicability; confirm against aircraft IPC/AMM/maintenance manual.
source: FAA Joint Aircraft System/Component Code Table and Definitions

title: Fuselage Internal Mounting Structure
definition: For reports of the internal mounting structure which aids in the support of the fuselage structure.
notes: Heuristic applicability; confirm against aircraft IPC/AMM/maintenance manual.
source: FAA Joint Aircraft System/Component Code Table and Definitions

title: Fuselage Internal Stairs
definition: For reports of the internal stairs which are part of the fuselage structure.
notes: Heuristic applicability; confirm against aircraft IPC/AMM/maintenance manual.
source: FAA Joint Aircraft System/Component Code Table and Definitions

title: Fuselage Fixed Partitions
definition: For reports of the fixed partitions which are part of the fuselage structure.
notes: Heuristic applicability; confirm against aircraft IPC/AMM/maintenance manual.
source: FAA Joint Aircraft System/Component Code Table and Definitions

title: Fuselage Main, Plate/Skin
definition: For reports of the exterior covering of the fuselage including access covers.
notes: Heuristic applicability; confirm against aircraft IPC/AMM/maintenance manual.
source: FAA Joint Aircraft System/Component Code Table and Definitions

title: Fuselage Main, Attach Fittings
definition: For reports of the fittings on the fuselage used for the attachment of doors, wings, stabilizers, landing gear, engine and rotor pylons, and the support of equipment within the fuselage. For reports of fuselage attach fittings that can not be specifically identified in JASC codes 5341 through 5347.
notes: Heuristic applicability; confirm against aircraft IPC/AMM/maintenance manual.
source: FAA Joint Aircraft System/Component Code Table and Definitions

title: Fuselage, Wing Attach Fittings
definition: For reports of the fittings on the fuselage used for the attachment of the wings.
notes: Heuristic applicability; confirm against aircraft IPC/AMM/maintenance manual.
source: FAA Joint Aircraft System/Component Code Table and Definitions

title: Fuselage, Stabilizer Attach Fittings
definition: For reports of the fittings on the fuselage used for the attachment of the stabilizers
notes: Heuristic applicability; confirm against aircraft IPC/AMM/maintenance manual.
source: FAA Joint Aircraft System/Component Code Table and Definitions

title: Fuselage, Landing Gear Attach Fittings
definition: For reports of the fittings on the fuselage used for the attachment of the landing gear.
notes: Heuristic applicability; confirm against aircraft IPC/AMM/maintenance manual.
source: FAA Joint Aircraft System/Component Code Table and Definitions

title: Fuselage, Door Hinge
definition: For reports of the fittings on the fuselage used for the attachment of the doors.
notes: Heuristic applicability; confirm against aircraft IPC/AMM/maintenance manual.
source: FAA Joint Aircraft System/Component Code Table and Definitions

title: Fuselage, Equipment Attach Fittings
definition: For reports of the fittings on the fuselage used for the attachment of equipment.
notes: Heuristic applicability; confirm against aircraft IPC/AMM/maintenance manual.
source: FAA Joint Aircraft System/Component Code Table and Definitions

title: Fuselage, Powerplant Attach Fittings
definition: For reports of the fittings on the fuselage used for the attachment of the powerplant. (i.e., the center engine on tri-engine airplanes).
notes: Heuristic applicability; confirm against aircraft IPC/AMM/maintenance manual.
source: FAA Joint Aircraft System/Component Code Table and Definitions

title: Fuselage, Seat/Cargo Attach Fittings
definition: For reports of the fittings on the fuselage used for the attachment of seats and cargo restraint mechanisms.
notes: Heuristic applicability; confirm against aircraft IPC/AMM/maintenance manual.
source: FAA Joint Aircraft System/Component Code Table and Definitions

title: Aerodynamic Fairings
definition: For reports of all fixed and removable aerodynamic fairings between the fuselage and wing/flap/empennage/pylon/nacelle attach points, tail cones and radomes. Also includes the fairings on rotorcraft tail cones. Typical parts are tail, radome, fairing, stiffener, skin, screw, fillet, etc.
notes: Heuristic applicability; confirm against aircraft IPC/AMM/maintenance manual.
source: FAA Joint Aircraft System/Component Code Table and Definitions

title: Fuselage Main, Bulkhead Fuselage wiring
definition: For reports indicating a problem with wiring specific to the Fuselage System.
notes: Heuristic applicability; confirm against aircraft IPC/AMM/maintenance manual.
source: FAA Joint Aircraft System/Component Code Table and Definitions

title: Nacelle/Pylon Structure
definition: For reports of structural units and associated components and members which furnish a means of mounting and housing the powerplant or rotor assembly. Includes the structure of powerplant cowling inclusive of the structural portion of the inlet whether or not integral with the aircraft. Structural portions of the exhaust system are excluded where they are not integral with the airframe. Use this code when there is insufficient information to file in a more specific JASC 5400 series code. NOTE: The use of this code should be avoided if possible as a reported defect is not likely to involve both pylon and nacelle.
notes: Heuristic applicability; confirm against aircraft IPC/AMM/maintenance manual.
source: FAA Joint Aircraft System/Component Code Table and Definitions

title: Nacelle/Pylon, Main Frame
definition: For reports of the structure which houses and supports powerplants. Includes the firewall and all structure aft on multi-engine aircraft and firewalls on single engine aircraft. Does not include engine mounting or cowling.
notes: Heuristic applicability; confirm against aircraft IPC/AMM/maintenance manual.
source: FAA Joint Aircraft System/Component Code Table and Definitions

title: Nacelle/Pylon, Frame/Spar/Rib
definition: For reports pertaining to the main frame, spar, or rib structure on the nacelles or pylons.
notes: Heuristic applicability; confirm against aircraft IPC/AMM/maintenance manual.
source: FAA Joint Aircraft System/Component Code Table and Definitions

title: Nacelle/Pylon, Bulkhead/Firewall
definition: For reports pertaining to the bulkhead or firewall structure on the nacelles or pylons which houses and supports the powerplants. Does not include the engine mounting or cowling. Typical parts are firewall, bulkhead, skin, stringer, beam, splice plate, etc.
notes: Heuristic applicability; confirm against aircraft IPC/AMM/maintenance manual.
source: FAA Joint Aircraft System/Component Code Table and Definitions

title: Nacelle/Pylon, Longeron/Stringer
definition: For reports pertaining to the longeron or stringer structure on the nacelles or pylons.
notes: Heuristic applicability; confirm against aircraft IPC/AMM/maintenance manual.
source: FAA Joint Aircraft System/Component Code Table and Definitions

title: Nacelle/Pylon, Plate/Skin
definition: For reports pertaining to the plates or skins on the nacelles or pylons.
notes: Heuristic applicability; confirm against aircraft IPC/AMM/maintenance manual.
source: FAA Joint Aircraft System/Component Code Table and Definitions

title: Nacelle/Pylon, Attach Fittings
definition: For reports on the fittings on the nacelles/pylons used for the attachment to its connecting structure, powerplant, thrust reverser, and for the support of equipment within the nacelle/pylon.
notes: Heuristic applicability; confirm against aircraft IPC/AMM/maintenance manual.
source: FAA Joint Aircraft System/Component Code Table and Definitions

title: Nacelle/Pylon Miscellaneous Structure
definition: For reports of miscellaneous structure on the nacelle/pylon structure which aids in the support of the primary structure. Includes such items as brackets, channels, stiffeners, doublers, clips, etc.
notes: Heuristic applicability; confirm against aircraft IPC/AMM/maintenance manual.
source: FAA Joint Aircraft System/Component Code Table and Definitions

title: Nacelle/Pylon System Wiring
definition: For reports indicating a problem with wiring specific to the Nacelle/Pylon System.
notes: Heuristic applicability; confirm against aircraft IPC/AMM/maintenance manual.
source: FAA Joint Aircraft System/Component Code Table and Definitions

title: Empennage Structure
definition: The horizontal and vertical stabilizers include the structure of the elevator and rudder. For general reports of empennage structure which affect two or more surfaces or are reported with insufficient information to file in a more specific JASC 5500 series code. This code is also used for reports that pertain to flying wires.
notes: Heuristic applicability; confirm against aircraft IPC/AMM/maintenance manual.
source: FAA Joint Aircraft System/Component Code Table and Definitions

title: Horizontal Stabilizer Structure
definition: For reports pertaining to the structural aspects of horizontal stabilizer and stabilators or canard. Includes fuselage and boom-to-surface attach fittings. Does not include actuating mechanism filed in JASC code 2742.
notes: Heuristic applicability; confirm against aircraft IPC/AMM/maintenance manual.
source: FAA Joint Aircraft System/Component Code Table and Definitions

title: Horizontal Stabilizer, Spar/Rib
definition: For specific reports of spars/ribs on the horizontal stabilizer.
notes: Heuristic applicability; confirm against aircraft IPC/AMM/maintenance manual.
source: FAA Joint Aircraft System/Component Code Table and Definitions

title: Horizontal Stabilizer, Plate/Skin
definition: For specific reports of plates/skins on the horizontal stabilizer.
notes: Heuristic applicability; confirm against aircraft IPC/AMM/maintenance manual.
source: FAA Joint Aircraft System/Component Code Table and Definitions

title: Horizontal Stabilizer, Tab Structure
definition: For reports pertaining to the structure and attachment of the tab surface mounted on movable stabilizers and stabilators. Includes hinge brackets and bearings/bushings. Does not include the actuating mechanism filed in JASC code 2740. Typical parts are hinge, skin, rib, spar, etc.
notes: Heuristic applicability; confirm against aircraft IPC/AMM/maintenance manual.
source: FAA Joint Aircraft System/Component Code Table and Definitions

title: Horizontal Stabilizer Miscellaneous Structure
definition: For reports of miscellaneous structure on the horizontal stabilizer structure which aids in the support of the primary structure. Includes such items as brackets, channels, stiffeners, doublers, clips, etc.
notes: Heuristic applicability; confirm against aircraft IPC/AMM/maintenance manual.
source: FAA Joint Aircraft System/Component Code Table and Definitions

title: Elevator Structure
definition: For reports pertaining to the structural aspects of the movable airfoil hinged to the horizontal stabilizer for longitudinal control. Includes the "ruddervator" on V-tail aircraft and balance weights. Does not include the stabilator structure which is filed in JASC code 5510 or the torque tubes which are filed under the actuating mechanism in JASC code 2730. Typical parts are hinge, hinge fittings, bearing, bolt, miscellaneous structure, etc.
notes: Heuristic applicability; confirm against aircraft IPC/AMM/maintenance manual.
source: FAA Joint Aircraft System/Component Code Table and Definitions

title: Elevator, Spar/Rib
definition: For specific reports of spars/ribs on the elevator.
notes: Heuristic applicability; confirm against aircraft IPC/AMM/maintenance manual.
source: FAA Joint Aircraft System/Component Code Table and Definitions

title: Elevator, Plate/Skin
definition: For specific reports of plates/skins on the elevator.
notes: Heuristic applicability; confirm against aircraft IPC/AMM/maintenance manual.
source: FAA Joint Aircraft System/Component Code Table and Definitions

title: Elevator, Tab Structure
definition: For reports pertaining to the structure of elevator trim surfaces hinged to elevators and "ruddervators." Includes hinge fittings and associated bearings and bolts. Does not include actuating mechanism filed in JASC code 2731.
notes: Heuristic applicability; confirm against aircraft IPC/AMM/maintenance manual.
source: FAA Joint Aircraft System/Component Code Table and Definitions

title: Elevator Miscellaneous Structure
definition: For reports of miscellaneous structure on the elevator structure which aids in the support of the primary structure. Includes such items as brackets, channels, stiffeners, doublers, clips, etc.
notes: Heuristic applicability; confirm against aircraft IPC/AMM/maintenance manual.
source: FAA Joint Aircraft System/Component Code Table and Definitions

title: Vertical Stabilizer
definition: The structural aspects of the fixed vertical surface attached to the fuselage including the dorsal fin.
notes: Heuristic applicability; confirm against aircraft IPC/AMM/maintenance manual.
source: FAA Joint Aircraft System/Component Code Table and Definitions

title: Vertical Stabilizer, Spar/Rib
definition: For specific reports of spars or ribs on the vertical stabilizer.
notes: Heuristic applicability; confirm against aircraft IPC/AMM/maintenance manual.
source: FAA Joint Aircraft System/Component Code Table and Definitions

title: Vertical Stabilizer, Plate/Skin
definition: For specific reports of plates or skins on the vertical stabilizer.
notes: Heuristic applicability; confirm against aircraft IPC/AMM/maintenance manual.
source: FAA Joint Aircraft System/Component Code Table and Definitions

title: Ventral Structure
definition: For reports pertaining to the ventral structure and skin of the ventral fin mounted on the lower, aft fuselage for added directional stability. Typical parts are skin, rib, rivet, and miscellaneous parts, etc.
notes: Heuristic applicability; confirm against aircraft IPC/AMM/maintenance manual.
source: FAA Joint Aircraft System/Component Code Table and Definitions

title: Vertical Stabilizer Miscellaneous Structure
definition: For reports of miscellaneous structure on the vertical stabilizer structure which aids in the support of the primary structure. Includes such items as brackets, channels, stiffeners, doublers, clips, etc.
notes: Heuristic applicability; confirm against aircraft IPC/AMM/maintenance manual.
source: FAA Joint Aircraft System/Component Code Table and Definitions

title: Rudder Structure
definition: For reports pertaining to the structural aspects of the vertical airfoil hinged to the vertical stabilizer. Does not include the actuators, actuator mechanism or mounting which are filed in JASC code 2720. Typical parts are hinge, hinge fittings, bearing, bolt, miscellaneous structure, etc.
notes: Heuristic applicability; confirm against aircraft IPC/AMM/maintenance manual.
source: FAA Joint Aircraft System/Component Code Table and Definitions

title: Rudder, Spar/Rib
definition: For specific reports pertaining to spars or ribs on the rudder structure.
notes: Heuristic applicability; confirm against aircraft IPC/AMM/maintenance manual.
source: FAA Joint Aircraft System/Component Code Table and Definitions

title: Rudder, Plate/Skin
definition: For specific reports pertaining to plates or skins on the rudder structure.
notes: Heuristic applicability; confirm against aircraft IPC/AMM/maintenance manual.
source: FAA Joint Aircraft System/Component Code Table and Definitions

title: Rudder, Tab Structure
definition: For reports pertaining to the structure of the movable surface hinged to the rudder surface for directional trim. Typical parts are skin, hinge fitting, spar, rib. The actuating mechanism is filed in JASC code 2720.
notes: Heuristic applicability; confirm against aircraft IPC/AMM/maintenance manual.
source: FAA Joint Aircraft System/Component Code Table and Definitions

title: Rudder Miscellaneous Structure
definition: For reports of miscellaneous structure on the rudder structure which aids in the support of the primary structure. Includes such items as brackets, channels, stiffeners, doublers, clips, etc.
notes: Heuristic applicability; confirm against aircraft IPC/AMM/maintenance manual.
source: FAA Joint Aircraft System/Component Code Table and Definitions

title: Empennage Flight Control Surfaces, Attach Fittings
definition: For miscellaneous reports of fittings on the empennage structure which are used for the support of the flight control, but are not specifically addressed in JASC codes 5551 through 5554.
notes: Heuristic applicability; confirm against aircraft IPC/AMM/maintenance manual.
source: FAA Joint Aircraft System/Component Code Table and Definitions

title: Horizontal Stabilizer, Attach Fittings
definition: For specific reports pertaining to the fittings on the horizontal stabilizer which are used to support equipment within the structure.
notes: Heuristic applicability; confirm against aircraft IPC/AMM/maintenance manual.
source: FAA Joint Aircraft System/Component Code Table and Definitions

title: Elevator Tab, Attach Fittings
definition: For specific reports pertaining to the fittings on the elevator or elevator tab which are used to support equipment within the structure.
notes: Heuristic applicability; confirm against aircraft IPC/AMM/maintenance manual.
source: FAA Joint Aircraft System/Component Code Table and Definitions

title: Vertical Stabilizer, Attach Fittings
definition: For specific reports pertaining to the fittings on the vertical stabilizer which are used to support equipment within the structure.
notes: Heuristic applicability; confirm against aircraft IPC/AMM/maintenance manual.
source: FAA Joint Aircraft System/Component Code Table and Definitions

title: Rudder/Tab, Attach Fittings
definition: For specific reports pertaining to the fittings on the rudder or rudder tab, which are used to support equipment within the structure.
notes: Heuristic applicability; confirm against aircraft IPC/AMM/maintenance manual.
source: FAA Joint Aircraft System/Component Code Table and Definitions

title: Stabilizer System Wiring
definition: For reports indicating a problem with wiring specific to the Stabilizer Systems.
notes: Heuristic applicability; confirm against aircraft IPC/AMM/maintenance manual.
source: FAA Joint Aircraft System/Component Code Table and Definitions

title: Window/Windshield System
definition: For reports pertaining to the fuselage and crew compartment windows inclusive of windshields. For reports of cockpit and cabin window or windshield defects reported with insufficient information to file in a specific JASC 5600 series code.
notes: Heuristic applicability; confirm against aircraft IPC/AMM/maintenance manual.
source: FAA Joint Aircraft System/Component Code Table and Definitions

title: Flight Compartment Windows
definition: For reports of all cockpit windows, cockpit overhead canopies, observation windows, and windshield panels in the flight compartment. Includes attachment and sliding feature of sliding windows. For cockpit windows including the breakage of electrically heated windshield panels regardless of cause. Does not include the heating aspects associated circuitry of heated windshields, which is filed in JASC code 3040. Typical parts are windshield, sliding window, seal, frame, panel, latch, hinge, chin bubbles, etc.
notes: Heuristic applicability; confirm against aircraft IPC/AMM/maintenance manual.
source: FAA Joint Aircraft System/Component Code Table and Definitions

title: Passenger Compartment Windows
definition: For reports of cabin mounted windows in the passenger compartments. Includes the inner and outer windows, frame attaching hardware, picture windows. Does not include the windows in the escape hatches which are filed in JASC code 5220.
notes: Heuristic applicability; confirm against aircraft IPC/AMM/maintenance manual.
source: FAA Joint Aircraft System/Component Code Table and Definitions

title: Door Windows
definition: For reports of windows mounted in doors. Does not include emergency exit windows which are filed in JASC code 5220.
notes: Heuristic applicability; confirm against aircraft IPC/AMM/maintenance manual.
source: FAA Joint Aircraft System/Component Code Table and Definitions

title: Inspection Windows
definition: For windows used for examining compartments and equipment in and about the aircraft such as door latches, and cargo bays.
notes: Heuristic applicability; confirm against aircraft IPC/AMM/maintenance manual.
source: FAA Joint Aircraft System/Component Code Table and Definitions

title: Window System Wiring
definition: For reports indicating a problem with wiring specific to the Window System.
notes: Heuristic applicability; confirm against aircraft IPC/AMM/maintenance manual.
source: FAA Joint Aircraft System/Component Code Table and Definitions

title: Wing Structure
definition: For reports pertaining to the center wing and outer wing structural units and associated components and members which support the aircraft in flight. This code should be used for general reports of the primary wing structure.
notes: Heuristic applicability; confirm against aircraft IPC/AMM/maintenance manual.
source: FAA Joint Aircraft System/Component Code Table and Definitions

title: Wing Main, Frame Structure
definition: For general reports of wing structure defects which affect two or more related parts or are reported with insufficient information to file in a more specific 5700 JASC code. Does not include reports pertaining to fuel tank sealing which are filed in JASC code 2810. Excessive use of this code should be avoided if possible.
notes: Heuristic applicability; confirm against aircraft IPC/AMM/maintenance manual.
source: FAA Joint Aircraft System/Component Code Table and Definitions

title: Wing Spar
definition: For reports pertaining to the spar in the wing structure.
notes: Heuristic applicability; confirm against aircraft IPC/AMM/maintenance manual.
source: FAA Joint Aircraft System/Component Code Table and Definitions

title: Wing, Rib/Bulkhead
definition: For reports pertaining to the ribs/bulkhead in the wing structure.
notes: Heuristic applicability; confirm against aircraft IPC/AMM/maintenance manual.
source: FAA Joint Aircraft System/Component Code Table and Definitions

title: Wing, Longeron/Stringer
definition: For reports pertaining to the longerons or stringers in the wing structure.
notes: Heuristic applicability; confirm against aircraft IPC/AMM/maintenance manual.
source: FAA Joint Aircraft System/Component Code Table and Definitions

title: Wing, Center Box
definition: For reports pertaining to the center wing box structure.
notes: Heuristic applicability; confirm against aircraft IPC/AMM/maintenance manual.
source: FAA Joint Aircraft System/Component Code Table and Definitions

title: Wing Miscellaneous Structure
definition: For reports of the auxiliary or miscellaneous wing structure. Includes the secondary items used for attachment. Does not include reports for plates or skins. Typical parts are wing tip, clips, brackets, channels, angles, stiffeners, doublers, etc.
