notes: Heuristic applicability; confirm against aircraft IPC/AMM/maintenance manual.
source: FAA Joint Aircraft System/Component Code Table and Definitions

title: Wing, Plate/Skin
definition: For reports of the exterior covering of the wing including the access covers, tip tank fillets, or fairings. Includes the leading edge and trailing edge skin and wing mounted fuel compartment panels.
notes: Heuristic applicability; confirm against aircraft IPC/AMM/maintenance manual.
source: FAA Joint Aircraft System/Component Code Table and Definitions

title: Wing, Attach Fittings
definition: The structure on the wing used for the attachment of fuselage, nacelle or pylon, and landing gear to the wing and for the support of equipment within the wing. Use this code for reports of wing attachments that can not be specifically identified in JASC codes 5741 through 5744. Does not include flight control or landing gear actuator support fittings which should be coded in the appropriate JASC 2700 or 3200 series code.
notes: Heuristic applicability; confirm against aircraft IPC/AMM/maintenance manual.
source: FAA Joint Aircraft System/Component Code Table and Definitions

title: Wing, Fuselage Attach Fittings
definition: The fittings on the wing used for attachment to the fuselage structure.
notes: Heuristic applicability; confirm against aircraft IPC/AMM/maintenance manual.
source: FAA Joint Aircraft System/Component Code Table and Definitions

title: Wing, Nacelle/Pylon Attach Fittings
definition: The fittings on the wing used for attachment to the nacelle/pylon.
notes: Heuristic applicability; confirm against aircraft IPC/AMM/maintenance manual.
source: FAA Joint Aircraft System/Component Code Table and Definitions

title: Wing, Landing Gear Attach Fittings
definition: The fittings on the wing used for attachment to the landing gear.
notes: Heuristic applicability; confirm against aircraft IPC/AMM/maintenance manual.
source: FAA Joint Aircraft System/Component Code Table and Definitions

title: Wing, Control Surface Attach Fittings
definition: The fittings on the wing used for attachment to the control surface.
notes: Heuristic applicability; confirm against aircraft IPC/AMM/maintenance manual.
source: FAA Joint Aircraft System/Component Code Table and Definitions

title: Wing, Control Surfaces
definition: For reports of a general nature involving the control surfaces which are attached to the wing.
notes: Heuristic applicability; confirm against aircraft IPC/AMM/maintenance manual.
source: FAA Joint Aircraft System/Component Code Table and Definitions

title: Ailerons
definition: For reports pertaining to the structural aspects of the aileron mounted on the trailing edge of wing. Includes hinges, balance weights. Does not include operating mechanism which causes the surface to move which is filed in JASC code 2710. Typical parts are skin, rib, spar, hinge, bracket, bolt, bearing, bushing, balance weight, etc.
notes: Heuristic applicability; confirm against aircraft IPC/AMM/maintenance manual.
source: FAA Joint Aircraft System/Component Code Table and Definitions

title: Aileron Tabs
definition: For reports pertaining to the structural aspects of the surface mounted at the trailing edge of the aileron for lateral trim. Does not include the operating mechanism filed in JASC code 2711. Typical parts are spar, skin, hinge, bracket, bolt, bearing, bushing.
notes: Heuristic applicability; confirm against aircraft IPC/AMM/maintenance manual.
source: FAA Joint Aircraft System/Component Code Table and Definitions

title: Trailing Edge Flaps
definition: For reports pertaining to the structural aspects of the flap surface mounted on the trailing edge of the wing (includes fore, mid, and aft segments). Does not include the operating mechanism such as the actuators, brackets, hydraulic or electric motors. Typical parts are skin, rib, spar, flap track, roller, flap carriage, bearing, bolt, rivet, etc.
notes: Heuristic applicability; confirm against aircraft IPC/AMM/maintenance manual.
source: FAA Joint Aircraft System/Component Code Table and Definitions

title: Leading Edge Devices
definition: For reports pertaining to the structural aspects of the wing leading edge device control surface. Includes hinge, brackets, bolts but does not include actuators or actuator mounting brackets which are filed in JASC code 2782. Typical parts are skin, rib, track, roller, bearing, carriage, etc.
notes: Heuristic applicability; confirm against aircraft IPC/AMM/maintenance manual.
source: FAA Joint Aircraft System/Component Code Table and Definitions

title: Spoilers
definition: For reports pertaining to the structural aspects of the movable surface on the upper surface of the wing for drag and lift reducing functions. Does not include operating mechanism such as actuators, hoses, lines which are filed in JASC codes 2760 and 2761. Typical parts are skin, rib, gusset plate, spoiler and actuator support fittings.
notes: Heuristic applicability; confirm against aircraft IPC/AMM/maintenance manual.
source: FAA Joint Aircraft System/Component Code Table and Definitions

title: Wing System Wiring
definition: For reports indicating a problem with wiring specific to the Wing Systems. PROPELLER/ROTOR SYSTEMS
notes: Heuristic applicability; confirm against aircraft IPC/AMM/maintenance manual.
source: FAA Joint Aircraft System/Component Code Table and Definitions

title: Propeller System
definition: The complete mechanical or electrical propeller, governor, alternators, pumps, motors those units and components external to or integral with the engine used to control the propeller blade angle. Includes the propulsor duct assemblies, aerodynamic fairing of mechanical components, stators, vectoring systems, etc. Use this code for reports with insufficient information to file in a more specific JASC 6100 series code.
notes: Heuristic applicability; confirm against aircraft IPC/AMM/maintenance manual.
source: FAA Joint Aircraft System/Component Code Table and Definitions

title: Propeller Assembly
definition: For reports of propeller assembly malfunctions excluding controlling aspects, with insufficient information to file in a more specific JASC 6100 series code, or for conditions which affect two or more parts of the propeller such as hub and blades. Includes the propeller retaining nut, etc.
notes: Heuristic applicability; confirm against aircraft IPC/AMM/maintenance manual.
source: FAA Joint Aircraft System/Component Code Table and Definitions

title: Propeller Blade Section
definition: For reports of blade defects other than deice boots. Includes retaining clamps and blade pitch change actuating mechanism which rotates with the propeller. Typical parts are blade, clamp, link, motor, counterweight, bearing, etc.
notes: Heuristic applicability; confirm against aircraft IPC/AMM/maintenance manual.
source: FAA Joint Aircraft System/Component Code Table and Definitions

title: Propeller Deice Boot Section
definition: For reports of defective deice/anti-ice system parts on the rotating parts of the propeller such as blades or spinner. Does not include the power source, controls or other non-rotating system parts in JASC code 3060. Typical parts are boot, cuff, heat element, slip ring, etc.
notes: Heuristic applicability; confirm against aircraft IPC/AMM/maintenance manual.
source: FAA Joint Aircraft System/Component Code Table and Definitions

title: Propeller Spinner Section
definition: For reports of defective propeller spinner assemblies. Typical parts are shell, backplate, bulkhead, rivets, screw, nut plate, brackets, etc.
notes: Heuristic applicability; confirm against aircraft IPC/AMM/maintenance manual.
source: FAA Joint Aircraft System/Component Code Table and Definitions

title: Propeller Hub Section
definition: For reports of defective hubs which house and support the rotating blades. Includes the dome, but not the blade actuating mechanism filed in JASC code 6111 or the attachment to engine flange in JASC code 6110.
notes: Heuristic applicability; confirm against aircraft IPC/AMM/maintenance manual.
source: FAA Joint Aircraft System/Component Code Table and Definitions

title: Propeller Controlling System
definition: For general reports of propeller speed controlling other than the governor unit or the synchronizer. Includes the controlling systems of propellers regardless of the propeller type. (Includes propeller regulator, negative torque switch, and the rigging mechanism). Also includes governor control linkage, levers, cable, and associated brackets from the cockpit to the governor; and the feather and unfeathering systems except the pump and accumulator. Typical parts are cable, bellcrank, lever, rod end, pressure switch, solenoid valve, beta switch, etc.
notes: Heuristic applicability; confirm against aircraft IPC/AMM/maintenance manual.
source: FAA Joint Aircraft System/Component Code Table and Definitions

title: Propeller Synchronizer Section
definition: The unit which controls the synchronization of propellers on multi-engine aircraft. Typical parts are synchronizer actuator, computer, synchrophaser, control unit, etc.
notes: Heuristic applicability; confirm against aircraft IPC/AMM/maintenance manual.
source: FAA Joint Aircraft System/Component Code Table and Definitions

title: Propeller Governor
definition: The unit which controls the propeller blade angle, but is limited to parts in and on the governor. Does not include airframe furnished control linkage from the cockpit which is filed in JASC code 6120. The component make and model should be included in the report as well as the number of the defective part. Typical parts are shaft, flyweight, governor, spring, arm, seal, beta valve, pilot valve, head, etc.
notes: Heuristic applicability; confirm against aircraft IPC/AMM/maintenance manual.
source: FAA Joint Aircraft System/Component Code Table and Definitions

title: Propeller Feathering/Reversing
definition: The component and parts which store and deliver an energy charge for propeller feathering and unfeathering. Includes the pump and associated motor, switch, circuitry and plumbing which provides the force for feathering the propeller blades for stopping the engine's rotation. Does not include propeller feathering system components which are coded in JASC code 6120, such as lever rod end, adjustment screw, solenoid, valves, etc. Typical parts are pump, motor, switch, accumulator, air valve, seal, etc.
notes: Heuristic applicability; confirm against aircraft IPC/AMM/maintenance manual.
source: FAA Joint Aircraft System/Component Code Table and Definitions

title: Propeller Braking
definition: The system components and parts which decrease run-down time or stop propeller rotation during engine power off conditions. This code is applicable to turboprop engines.
notes: Heuristic applicability; confirm against aircraft IPC/AMM/maintenance manual.
source: FAA Joint Aircraft System/Component Code Table and Definitions

title: Propeller Indication System
definition: The system components and parts which indicates the operation or activation of propeller systems. Typical parts are switch, lamp, connector, harness, indicator, etc.
notes: Heuristic applicability; confirm against aircraft IPC/AMM/maintenance manual.
source: FAA Joint Aircraft System/Component Code Table and Definitions

title: Propeller/Propulsor System Wiring
definition: For reports indicating a problem with wiring specific to the Propeller/Propulsor Systems.
notes: Heuristic applicability; confirm against aircraft IPC/AMM/maintenance manual.
source: FAA Joint Aircraft System/Component Code Table and Definitions

title: Main Rotor System
definition: For general reports of rotorcraft main rotor systems with insufficient information to file in a more specific JASC 6200 series code. Excessive use of this code should be avoided if possible.
notes: Heuristic applicability; confirm against aircraft IPC/AMM/maintenance manual.
source: FAA Joint Aircraft System/Component Code Table and Definitions

title: Main Rotor Blades
definition: For reports of defective blades including attachment to the rotor head and heating mats on the blades for anti-icing. Also includes tilt rotor blades. Does not include the anti-icing system which is filed in JASC code 3060, or the rotor head which is filed in JASC code 6220. Typical parts are blade, attach bolt, bushing, etc.
notes: Heuristic applicability; confirm against aircraft IPC/AMM/maintenance manual.
source: FAA Joint Aircraft System/Component Code Table and Definitions

title: Main Rotor Head
definition: The rotating assembly which supports the main rotor blades including blade folding system. Includes the swashplate if it is an integral part of the mast head assembly. Also includes the head mechanism on tilt rotor aircraft. Typical parts are sleeve, spindle, damper, fairing. Does not include the controlling aspects in JASC code 6710.
notes: Heuristic applicability; confirm against aircraft IPC/AMM/maintenance manual.
source: FAA Joint Aircraft System/Component Code Table and Definitions

title: Main Rotor Mast/Swashplate
definition: The vertical shaft which supports the main rotor head. Typical parts are shaft, bearing, guide, mast, seal, swashplate, etc.
notes: Heuristic applicability; confirm against aircraft IPC/AMM/maintenance manual.
source: FAA Joint Aircraft System/Component Code Table and Definitions

title: Main Rotor Indicating System
definition: The system used to indicate the operation or activation of the main rotor. Includes lights, gauges, switches, wiring, etc.
notes: Heuristic applicability; confirm against aircraft IPC/AMM/maintenance manual.
source: FAA Joint Aircraft System/Component Code Table and Definitions

title: Main Rotor System Wiring
definition: For reports indicating a problem with wiring specific to the Main Rotor System.
notes: Heuristic applicability; confirm against aircraft IPC/AMM/maintenance manual.
source: FAA Joint Aircraft System/Component Code Table and Definitions

title: Main Rotor Drive System
definition: For general reports of main rotor drive system with insufficient information to file in a more specific JASC sub-system code. Excessive use of this code should be avoided if possible.
notes: Heuristic applicability; confirm against aircraft IPC/AMM/maintenance manual.
source: FAA Joint Aircraft System/Component Code Table and Definitions

title: Engine/Transmission Coupling
definition: The drive shaft between the engine and the main gearbox including the clutch and freewheel units (if applicable), and tilt rotor interconnect system. Typical parts are clutch, shaft, coupling, bearing, boot, seal, sync shaft, pulley, pulley bracket, belt, etc.
notes: Heuristic applicability; confirm against aircraft IPC/AMM/maintenance manual.
source: FAA Joint Aircraft System/Component Code Table and Definitions

title: Main Rotor Gearbox
definition: The component which transmits engine power to rotary motion in the main rotor mast. Includes mechanical power take-off and accessory drives but does not include the accessories such as hydraulic pumps and alternators. Includes gearbox lubricating system. Typical parts are gearbox, case, shaft, gear, pump, seal, sun gear, etc.
notes: Heuristic applicability; confirm against aircraft IPC/AMM/maintenance manual.
source: FAA Joint Aircraft System/Component Code Table and Definitions

title: Main Rotor Brake
definition: The system which reduces rundown time or stops rotor rotation during engine power off conditions. Typical parts are brake, caliper, lining, seal, check valve, etc.
notes: Heuristic applicability; confirm against aircraft IPC/AMM/maintenance manual.
source: FAA Joint Aircraft System/Component Code Table and Definitions

title: Rotorcraft Cooling Fan System
definition: The component which provides a cooling air flow to the rotorcraft piston engine cylinders, oil coolers, etc. Typical parts are fan, shroud, blade, impeller, duct, drive belt, stator, etc.
notes: Heuristic applicability; confirm against aircraft IPC/AMM/maintenance manual.
source: FAA Joint Aircraft System/Component Code Table and Definitions

title: Main Rotor Transmission Mount
definition: The suspension system for the transmission mounting in airframe. Typical parts are suspension bars, isolation mount, etc.
notes: Heuristic applicability; confirm against aircraft IPC/AMM/maintenance manual.
source: FAA Joint Aircraft System/Component Code Table and Definitions

title: Rotor Drive Indicating System
definition: The indicators, sensors/transmitters and associated systems which indicate operation or activation of rotor systems. Typical parts are tachometer, transmitter, circuit breaker, wiring harness, light, switch, indicator, needle, etc.
notes: Heuristic applicability; confirm against aircraft IPC/AMM/maintenance manual.
source: FAA Joint Aircraft System/Component Code Table and Definitions

title: Main Rotor Drive System Wiring
definition: For reports indicating a problem with wiring specific to the Main Rotor Drive System.
notes: Heuristic applicability; confirm against aircraft IPC/AMM/maintenance manual.
source: FAA Joint Aircraft System/Component Code Table and Definitions

title: Tail Rotor System
definition: For general reports of the rotorcraft tail rotor system reported with insufficient information to file in a more specific tail rotor system. Avoid excessive use of this code if possible.
notes: Heuristic applicability; confirm against aircraft IPC/AMM/maintenance manual.
source: FAA Joint Aircraft System/Component Code Table and Definitions

title: Tail Rotor Blades
definition: For reports of defective tail rotor blades including heating mats for anti-icing but not the anti-icing system in JASC code 3060. Also includes attachment to rotor head. Typical parts are blade, attach bolt, etc.
notes: Heuristic applicability; confirm against aircraft IPC/AMM/maintenance manual.
source: FAA Joint Aircraft System/Component Code Table and Definitions

title: Tail Rotor Head
definition: The rotating assembly which supports the tail rotor blades. Does not include the controlling aspects which are filed in JASC code 6720. Typical parts are trunnion, fairing, damper plate, shaft, hub, etc.
notes: Heuristic applicability; confirm against aircraft IPC/AMM/maintenance manual.
source: FAA Joint Aircraft System/Component Code Table and Definitions

title: Tail Rotor Indicating System
definition: The indicators, sensors, transmitters, and associated systems which indicates operation or activation of the tail rotor system.
notes: Heuristic applicability; confirm against aircraft IPC/AMM/maintenance manual.
source: FAA Joint Aircraft System/Component Code Table and Definitions

title: Tail Rotor System Wiring
definition: For reports indicating a problem with wiring specific to the Tail Rotor System.
notes: Heuristic applicability; confirm against aircraft IPC/AMM/maintenance manual.
source: FAA Joint Aircraft System/Component Code Table and Definitions

title: Tail Rotor Drive System
definition: For general reports of the tail rotor drive system reported with insufficient information to file in a more specific tail rotor drive system. Avoid excessive use of this code if possible.
notes: Heuristic applicability; confirm against aircraft IPC/AMM/maintenance manual.
source: FAA Joint Aircraft System/Component Code Table and Definitions

title: Tail Rotor Drive Shaft
definition: The shafts, flexible couplings and bearings, etc., from the main rotor transmission to the tail rotor assembly. Typical parts shaft, coupling, bearing, hanger, etc.
notes: Heuristic applicability; confirm against aircraft IPC/AMM/maintenance manual.
source: FAA Joint Aircraft System/Component Code Table and Definitions

title: Tail Rotor Gearbox
definition: The gearboxes which transmit engine power to the tail rotor. Includes intermediate gearboxes. Typical parts are case, seal, box, gear, spider gear, gearbox cowling and fairing, etc.
notes: Heuristic applicability; confirm against aircraft IPC/AMM/maintenance manual.
source: FAA Joint Aircraft System/Component Code Table and Definitions

title: Tail Rotor Drive Indicating System
definition: The indicators, sensors, transmitters, and associated systems which indicates operation or activation of the tail rotor drive system.
notes: Heuristic applicability; confirm against aircraft IPC/AMM/maintenance manual.
source: FAA Joint Aircraft System/Component Code Table and Definitions

title: Tail Rotor Drive System Wiring
definition: For reports indicating a problem with wiring specific to the Tail Rotor Drive System.
notes: Heuristic applicability; confirm against aircraft IPC/AMM/maintenance manual.
source: FAA Joint Aircraft System/Component Code Table and Definitions

title: Rotorcraft Flight Control
definition: For general reports of rotorcraft control systems with insufficient information to file in a more specific 6700 JASC series code; or for reports which affect both main and tail rotor control systems. Avoid excessive use of this code if possible.
notes: Heuristic applicability; confirm against aircraft IPC/AMM/maintenance manual.
source: FAA Joint Aircraft System/Component Code Table and Definitions

title: Main Rotor Control
definition: The system components and parts other than the servo control system which control and indicates the attitude or the angle of attack of the main rotor blades. Typical parts are collective pitch lever, cyclic pitch stick, coupling and mixing units, and position indicators, etc.
notes: Heuristic applicability; confirm against aircraft IPC/AMM/maintenance manual.
source: FAA Joint Aircraft System/Component Code Table and Definitions

title: Tilt Rotor Flight Control
definition: The system components and parts of the tilt rotor control system which controls the attitude of the aircraft by rotating the dual main rotor assembly through a 90-degree position. The zero or vertical position allows vertical takeoff and landing of the aircraft.
notes: Heuristic applicability; confirm against aircraft IPC/AMM/maintenance manual.
source: FAA Joint Aircraft System/Component Code Table and Definitions

title: Tail Rotor Control System
definition: The components and system parts which control movement about the vertical axis. The directional control may be accomplished by changing the tail rotor blade angle, or by directed compressed air (i.e., NOTAR systems). Includes tail rotor control pedals, cables, rods, bellcranks, associated support brackets, compressed air ducts, valves, etc.
notes: Heuristic applicability; confirm against aircraft IPC/AMM/maintenance manual.
source: FAA Joint Aircraft System/Component Code Table and Definitions

title: Rotorcraft Servo System
definition: The system which ensures distribution of mechanical or electrical power to the rotor servo-control system. Includes systems used to monitor and indicate operation of the servo control system. Typical parts are pressure relief valves, electro valves, check valves, accumulators, etc.
notes: Heuristic applicability; confirm against aircraft IPC/AMM/maintenance manual.
source: FAA Joint Aircraft System/Component Code Table and Definitions

title: Rotors Flight Control System Wiring
definition: For reports indicating a problem with wiring specific to the Rotors Flight Control System. POWERPLANT SYSTEMS
notes: Heuristic applicability; confirm against aircraft IPC/AMM/maintenance manual.
source: FAA Joint Aircraft System/Component Code Table and Definitions

title: Powerplant System
definition: For general reports concerning the powerplant package. Avoid the use of this code if enough information is provided to file in a more specific JASC 7100 series code.
notes: Heuristic applicability; confirm against aircraft IPC/AMM/maintenance manual.
source: FAA Joint Aircraft System/Component Code Table and Definitions

title: Engine Cowling System
definition: The enclosure which houses engines for drag reducing and cooling. Includes attachment, structure and access doors. Does not include engine cylinder baffles of fire seals. Typical parts are latch, fastener, lockpin, hook, skin, nose cap, stud, access door, hinge, hinge pin, rivet, bracket, stiffener, etc.
notes: Heuristic applicability; confirm against aircraft IPC/AMM/maintenance manual.
source: FAA Joint Aircraft System/Component Code Table and Definitions

title: Engine Cowl Flaps
definition: The flaps mounted in engine cowling for increased cooling air flow. Also includes the component which electrically or hydraulically actuates the cowl flaps. Typical parts are actuator, piston, seal, hinge bracket, skin, doubler, rod, rod end, lever, rivet, bolt, and flap, etc.
notes: Heuristic applicability; confirm against aircraft IPC/AMM/maintenance manual.
source: FAA Joint Aircraft System/Component Code Table and Definitions

title: Engine Air Baffle Section
definition: For reports of baffles which direct cooling air flow to the engine cylinders and accessories. Does not include cylinder baffles certificated with the engine which are filed in JASC code 8530. Typical parts are baffle, shield, bracket, shroud, cooling ducts for starters, and generators, etc.
notes: Heuristic applicability; confirm against aircraft IPC/AMM/maintenance manual.
source: FAA Joint Aircraft System/Component Code Table and Definitions

title: Engine Mount Section
definition: The structural framework which supports the engine on the nacelle, firewall or pylon. Typical parts are mount, bracket, fitting, shock mount, bolt, isolator, hanger, etc.
notes: Heuristic applicability; confirm against aircraft IPC/AMM/maintenance manual.
source: FAA Joint Aircraft System/Component Code Table and Definitions

title: Engine Fireseals
definition: The fire-resistant partitions and seals mounted on or about the power package to isolate areas subject to fire. Does not include firewalls which are filed in JASC code 5412. Typical parts are shroud, bracket, etc.
notes: Heuristic applicability; confirm against aircraft IPC/AMM/maintenance manual.
source: FAA Joint Aircraft System/Component Code Table and Definitions

title: Engine Air Intake System
definition: The portion of the powerplant system which directs airflow to the engine. Does not include integral structure with the airframe, which shall be included in the applicable structures JASC chapter. Typical parts are carburetor air heat doors, alternate air doors, linkages, controls, filter element, ducts, hose, air box, latch, seals, nose ring cowls, scoops, compressor fan cowls, compressor fan case, buried engine ducts, vortex generators, actuators, control handles, cables, wiring, plumbing, doors, warning systems, position indicators, etc.
notes: Heuristic applicability; confirm against aircraft IPC/AMM/maintenance manual.
source: FAA Joint Aircraft System/Component Code Table and Definitions

title: Engine Drains
definition: The components and manifold assemblies which are used to drain off excess fluids from the powerplant and its accessories. Includes components that are integral parts of, or fitted to the powerplant cowling. Typical parts are drain line, manifold, flame arrestors, support brackets, etc.
notes: Heuristic applicability; confirm against aircraft IPC/AMM/maintenance manual.
source: FAA Joint Aircraft System/Component Code Table and Definitions

title: Powerplant System Wiring
definition: For reports indicating a problem with wiring specific to the Powerplant System.
notes: Heuristic applicability; confirm against aircraft IPC/AMM/maintenance manual.
source: FAA Joint Aircraft System/Component Code Table and Definitions

title: Engine (Turbine/Turboprop)
definition: The units and components which are used to induce and convert fuel-air mixture into power, and transmit power to the propeller shaft (if any) and accessory drives. Full identification of the powerplant is required in all reports in JASC series codes 7200 through 8300 to increase the usability of the reports. Use this code for general reports concerning engine problems reported with insufficient information to file in a more specific JASC code. Includes reports pertaining to bird strikes to engines/cowling.
notes: Heuristic applicability; confirm against aircraft IPC/AMM/maintenance manual.
source: FAA Joint Aircraft System/Component Code Table and Definitions

title: Turbine Engine Reduction Gear
definition: For reports pertaining to reduction gears, combining gearboxes, propeller drive shafts, and helicopter rotor shafts, which are used to transfer power from turboprop and turboshaft engines, to the propeller or helicopter rotor. Do not use this code for accessory devices attached to reduction gearboxes. Typical parts are shaft, gear, bearing, case, torque piston, transfer tube, chip detector, etc.
notes: Heuristic applicability; confirm against aircraft IPC/AMM/maintenance manual.
source: FAA Joint Aircraft System/Component Code Table and Definitions

title: Turbine Engine Air Inlet Section
definition: The engine section through which air enters the compressor section. Typical parts are inlet case, inlet cone, inlet screen, guide vane, inlet scroll, etc.
notes: Heuristic applicability; confirm against aircraft IPC/AMM/maintenance manual.
source: FAA Joint Aircraft System/Component Code Table and Definitions

title: Turbine Engine Compressor Section
definition: The engine section where incoming air is compressed. Includes the operation of variable stator blades, linkage to the various valves and sense lines. Typical parts are case, the rotating portion of the compressor, lines, fan blades, disc, bearing, seal, mount, carbon seal, disc tie bolts, shaft, static and variable stator blades, linkage, actuator, etc.
notes: Heuristic applicability; confirm against aircraft IPC/AMM/maintenance manual.
source: FAA Joint Aircraft System/Component Code Table and Definitions

title: Turbine Engine Combustion Section
definition: The engine section in which fuel and air are mixed and burned. Typical parts are case, burner can, liner, vane ring, etc.
notes: Heuristic applicability; confirm against aircraft IPC/AMM/maintenance manual.
source: FAA Joint Aircraft System/Component Code Table and Definitions

title: Turbine Section
definition: The engine section which contains the turbine disc and associated nozzles and cases. Typical parts are case, disc, blade, nozzle, bearing, bearing cover, power turbine, shaft, tie bolts, seals, etc.
notes: Heuristic applicability; confirm against aircraft IPC/AMM/maintenance manual.
source: FAA Joint Aircraft System/Component Code Table and Definitions

title: Turbine Engine Accessory Drive
definition: The engine mounted gearbox which provides mechanical power takeoffs to drive accessories such as pumps, generators, chip detectors. Does not include the remote gearboxes which are filed in JASC code 8300.
notes: Heuristic applicability; confirm against aircraft IPC/AMM/maintenance manual.
source: FAA Joint Aircraft System/Component Code Table and Definitions

title: Turbine Engine Oil System
definition: The system components and parts which provide lubricating oil pressure, circulation and scavenging throughout the engine. Does not include externally mounted storage tanks filed in JASC code 7910, coolers in JASC code 7921, or connecting lines in JASC code 7920. Typical parts are relief valve, fitting, seal, pump, screen, filter, seal, check valve, element, etc.
notes: Heuristic applicability; confirm against aircraft IPC/AMM/maintenance manual.
source: FAA Joint Aircraft System/Component Code Table and Definitions

title: Turbine Engine Bypass Section
definition: For the non-rotating portion of engine air flow ducting for the prime purpose of adding to engine thrust of turbo-jet engines. Does not include the rotating components such as blades. Typical parts are duct, skin, duct segment, etc.
notes: Heuristic applicability; confirm against aircraft IPC/AMM/maintenance manual.
source: FAA Joint Aircraft System/Component Code Table and Definitions

title: Turbine Engine System Wiring
definition: For reports indicating a problem with wiring specific to the Turbine Engine System.
notes: Heuristic applicability; confirm against aircraft IPC/AMM/maintenance manual.
source: FAA Joint Aircraft System/Component Code Table and Definitions

title: Engine Fuel and Control
definition: For general reports of turbine or reciprocating engine fuel systems with insufficient information to file in a more specific JASC 7300 series code.
notes: Heuristic applicability; confirm against aircraft IPC/AMM/maintenance manual.
source: FAA Joint Aircraft System/Component Code Table and Definitions

title: Engine Fuel Distribution
definition: For components and parts of the engine fuel system from the main quick disconnect fitting or airframe fuel system strainer to the fuel control unit. Does not include the controlling or metering aspects filed in JASC code 7322, or the primer systems in JASC code 2820 on reciprocating engines, or the engine fuel pumps, fuel heater, cooler, divider, or injector nozzle (turbine and piston engines). Typical parts are supply lines, hoses, fuel, filters on turbine engines, shutoff and solenoid valves, etc.
notes: Heuristic applicability; confirm against aircraft IPC/AMM/maintenance manual.
source: FAA Joint Aircraft System/Component Code Table and Definitions

title: Engine Fuel/Oil Cooler
definition: The unit in which aircraft fuel flows to cool the turbine engine lubricating oil. Does not include the connecting lines.
notes: Heuristic applicability; confirm against aircraft IPC/AMM/maintenance manual.
source: FAA Joint Aircraft System/Component Code Table and Definitions

title: Fuel Heater
definition: The unit which heats fuel flowing to the engine to prevent freezing of entrapped water. Does not include connecting lines or the heat source.
notes: Heuristic applicability; confirm against aircraft IPC/AMM/maintenance manual.
source: FAA Joint Aircraft System/Component Code Table and Definitions

title: Fuel Injector Nozzle
definition: The unit which injects metered fuel into piston engine cylinders and burner cans in turbine engines.
notes: Heuristic applicability; confirm against aircraft IPC/AMM/maintenance manual.
source: FAA Joint Aircraft System/Component Code Table and Definitions

title: Engine Fuel Pump
definition: For reports pertaining to engine fuel pumps. Typical parts are housing, spring, rocker, pump, diaphragm, shaft, seal, relief valve, regulator, coupling, etc.
notes: Heuristic applicability; confirm against aircraft IPC/AMM/maintenance manual.
source: FAA Joint Aircraft System/Component Code Table and Definitions

title: Fuel Controlling System
definition: The system components or parts other than the fuel control, amplifier, computer, carburetor and indication systems which control and deliver metered fuel/air to engine cylinders or turbine engine burner cans. Typical parts are sense line, power and drain valve (P & D valve), drain valve, carburetor inlet temperature sensor, etc.
notes: Heuristic applicability; confirm against aircraft IPC/AMM/maintenance manual.
source: FAA Joint Aircraft System/Component Code Table and Definitions

title: Fuel Control/Turbine Engines
definition: The components which electronically control metered fuel flow under infinite temperature, altitude, and barometric pressure conditions. This code is also to be used for turbine engines which utilize electronic and non-electronic fuel controls. Typical parts are computer, amplifier, sync box, CIT sensor, etc.
notes: Heuristic applicability; confirm against aircraft IPC/AMM/maintenance manual.
source: FAA Joint Aircraft System/Component Code Table and Definitions

title: Fuel Control/Reciprocating Engines
definition: The component which meters fuel/air mixture for engine combustion on reciprocating engines. This code is to be used for fuel injection systems, carburetor systems or other mechanical fuel metering devices reciprocating engine. The typical parts are carburetor, fuel injector, float, diaphragm, bellows, shaft, venturi, control arm, throttle body, servo, discharge tube, bushing, needle, seat, accelerator pump, pin, bearings, etc.
notes: Heuristic applicability; confirm against aircraft IPC/AMM/maintenance manual.
source: FAA Joint Aircraft System/Component Code Table and Definitions

title: Turbine Governor
definition: The component which controls the RPM of turbine engines. Typical parts are governor, shaft, overspeed limiter, topping governor, etc.
notes: Heuristic applicability; confirm against aircraft IPC/AMM/maintenance manual.
source: FAA Joint Aircraft System/Component Code Table and Definitions

title: Fuel Divider
definition: The unit in metered fuel lines which directs fuel to individual cylinders or burner cans.
notes: Heuristic applicability; confirm against aircraft IPC/AMM/maintenance manual.
source: FAA Joint Aircraft System/Component Code Table and Definitions

title: Engine Fuel Indicating System
definition: For reports of fuel temperature, flow rate, or pressure indicating and warning systems other than the indicators, sensors, and transmitters. Typical parts are line, hose, lamp, bulb, wiring harness, circuit breaker, etc.
notes: Heuristic applicability; confirm against aircraft IPC/AMM/maintenance manual.
source: FAA Joint Aircraft System/Component Code Table and Definitions

title: Fuel Flow Indicating
definition: The instrument which indicates the flow rate of metered fuel to the engine. Does not include the transmitter. Typical parts are indicator, power supply, needle, dial, etc.
notes: Heuristic applicability; confirm against aircraft IPC/AMM/maintenance manual.
source: FAA Joint Aircraft System/Component Code Table and Definitions

title: Fuel Pressure Indicating
definition: The instrument which indicates the pressure of fuel at the fuel control/carburetor as provided by the engine driven or motor driven pumps. Includes the pressure warning indicating lamps. Typical parts are indicator, bourdon tube, diaphragm, needle, case, etc.
notes: Heuristic applicability; confirm against aircraft IPC/AMM/maintenance manual.
source: FAA Joint Aircraft System/Component Code Table and Definitions

title: Fuel Flow Sensor
definition: The unit and associated circuitry and parts which senses and transmits the rate of fuel flow to the cockpit indicator. Typical parts are transmitter, sensor, fitting, connector, transducer, etc.
notes: Heuristic applicability; confirm against aircraft IPC/AMM/maintenance manual.
source: FAA Joint Aircraft System/Component Code Table and Definitions

title: Fuel Pressure Sensor
definition: The units which sense and transmit to the cockpit indicator or indicator lamps, the pressure of fuel available at the engine fuel control/carburetor. Includes pressure switch and circuitry for warning indication. Typical parts are transducer, transmitter, switch, etc.
notes: Heuristic applicability; confirm against aircraft IPC/AMM/maintenance manual.
source: FAA Joint Aircraft System/Component Code Table and Definitions

title: Engine Fuel System Wiring
definition: For reports indicating a problem with wiring specific to the Engine Fuel System.
notes: Heuristic applicability; confirm against aircraft IPC/AMM/maintenance manual.
source: FAA Joint Aircraft System/Component Code Table and Definitions

title: Ignition System
definition: For general reports of ignition problems with insufficient information to file in a more specific JASC 7400 series code.
notes: Heuristic applicability; confirm against aircraft IPC/AMM/maintenance manual.
source: FAA Joint Aircraft System/Component Code Table and Definitions

title: Ignition Power Supply
definition: The units and components which generate, control, furnish or distribute an electrical current to ignite the fuel air mixture in cylinders of reciprocating engines or in the combustion chambers or thrust augmentors of turbine engines.
notes: Heuristic applicability; confirm against aircraft IPC/AMM/maintenance manual.
source: FAA Joint Aircraft System/Component Code Table and Definitions

title: Low Tension Coil
definition: For reports of magneto coils used on select engines such as the Pratt & Whitney (PWA), Model R2800, to generate a low tension voltage to high tension voltage coil mounted at each engine cylinder. Not generally used on modern light aircraft reciprocating engines.
notes: Heuristic applicability; confirm against aircraft IPC/AMM/maintenance manual.
source: FAA Joint Aircraft System/Component Code Table and Definitions

title: Exciter
definition: The unit used with turbine engine ignition systems for starting engines. Typical parts are exciter box, bracket, relay. The component make and model should be included.
notes: Heuristic applicability; confirm against aircraft IPC/AMM/maintenance manual.
source: FAA Joint Aircraft System/Component Code Table and Definitions

title: Induction Vibrator
definition: The unit which provides a high tension spark to reciprocating engine spark plugs for starting.
notes: Heuristic applicability; confirm against aircraft IPC/AMM/maintenance manual.
source: FAA Joint Aircraft System/Component Code Table and Definitions

title: Magneto/Distributor
definition: The components which generate and distribute a high voltage to spark plugs in reciprocating engines for fuel/air combustion. Typical parts are coil, breaker points, gear, bearing, contact finger, distributor block, frame, impulse coupling, condenser, rotor, cam, electrode, seal, etc.
notes: Heuristic applicability; confirm against aircraft IPC/AMM/maintenance manual.
source: FAA Joint Aircraft System/Component Code Table and Definitions

title: Ignition Harness (Distribution)
definition: The high tension insulated wiring from the magneto to the spark plug in reciprocating engines which provides a spark for combustion. For turbine engine, the high tension leads to burner can igniters for used for starting. Typical parts are lead, shielding, sleeve, ignition cable, terminal, ferrule, etc.
notes: Heuristic applicability; confirm against aircraft IPC/AMM/maintenance manual.
source: FAA Joint Aircraft System/Component Code Table and Definitions

title: Spark Plug/Igniter
definition: The part which provides the spark in the reciprocating engine cylinders or combustion chamber of turbine engines.
notes: Heuristic applicability; confirm against aircraft IPC/AMM/maintenance manual.
source: FAA Joint Aircraft System/Component Code Table and Definitions

title: Ignition Switching
definition: The unit which provides a means of rendering the ignition power supply (magneto) inoperative. Also used to direct electrical current to the magneto switch. Does not include engine cranking which is covered in JASC code 8010. Typical parts are start button (only if associated with a ignition switching discrepancy), switch, back plate, contacts, etc.
notes: Heuristic applicability; confirm against aircraft IPC/AMM/maintenance manual.
source: FAA Joint Aircraft System/Component Code Table and Definitions

title: Ignition System Wiring
definition: For reports indicating a problem with wiring specific to the Ignition System.
notes: Heuristic applicability; confirm against aircraft IPC/AMM/maintenance manual.
source: FAA Joint Aircraft System/Component Code Table and Definitions

title: Engine Bleed Air System
definition: For general reports of turbine engine compressor bleed air systems used to control the flow of air through the engine, cooling air systems, and heated air for engine anti-icing reported with insufficient information to file in a more specific JASC 7500 series code.
notes: Heuristic applicability; confirm against aircraft IPC/AMM/maintenance manual.
source: FAA Joint Aircraft System/Component Code Table and Definitions

title: Engine Anti-Icing System
definition: The engine system components and parts used to eliminate and prevent the formation of ice. Includes the control valve and associated actuator, switch and circuitry which controls the flow of turbine engine compressor bleed air to the engine anti-icing system. Anti-icing reports pertaining to the powerplant cowling are filed in JASC code 3020. Typical parts are control valve, actuator, motor, switch, relay, circuit breaker, hose, manifold, coupling, fuel heat duct, fuel heat valve, etc.
notes: Heuristic applicability; confirm against aircraft IPC/AMM/maintenance manual.
source: FAA Joint Aircraft System/Component Code Table and Definitions

title: Engine Cooling System
definition: The portion of the engine compressor bleed air system which is used to ventilate engine compartments and accessories. Does not include the engine bleed control valve which is filed in JASC code 7532. Typical parts are jet pumps, vortex generators, valve, actuator, and associated parts and circuitry used to control bleed air to engine accessory cooling systems.
notes: Heuristic applicability; confirm against aircraft IPC/AMM/maintenance manual.
source: FAA Joint Aircraft System/Component Code Table and Definitions

title: Compressor Bleed Control
definition: The system except valve and governor which controls the flow of air through turbine engines. Typical parts are sense line, fittings, cables, sense line filter, speed sense valve, etc.
notes: Heuristic applicability; confirm against aircraft IPC/AMM/maintenance manual.
source: FAA Joint Aircraft System/Component Code Table and Definitions

title: Compressor Bleed Governor
definition: The unit controlling relative position of the compressor bleed valve in turbine engines for air flow control.
notes: Heuristic applicability; confirm against aircraft IPC/AMM/maintenance manual.
source: FAA Joint Aircraft System/Component Code Table and Definitions

title: Compressor Bleed Valve
definition: The component which releases air from turbine engine compressor sections for air flow control. Typical parts are bleed valve, actuator, check valve, etc.
notes: Heuristic applicability; confirm against aircraft IPC/AMM/maintenance manual.
source: FAA Joint Aircraft System/Component Code Table and Definitions

title: Bleed Air Indicating System
definition: The systems which indicate temperature, pressure, control positions and warning indications of turbine engine compressor bleed air systems in turbine engines. Typical parts are transmitter, sensor, indicator, lamp, pressure switch, etc.
notes: Heuristic applicability; confirm against aircraft IPC/AMM/maintenance manual.
source: FAA Joint Aircraft System/Component Code Table and Definitions

title: Engine Bleed Air System Wiring
definition: For reports indicating a problem with wiring specific to the Engine Bleed Air System.
notes: Heuristic applicability; confirm against aircraft IPC/AMM/maintenance manual.
source: FAA Joint Aircraft System/Component Code Table and Definitions

title: Engine Controls
definition: The controls which govern the operation of the engine. Includes units and components which are interconnected for emergency shutdown. For turboprop engines, includes linkages and controls to the coordinator or equivalent to the propeller governor, fuel control unit or other units being controlled. For reciprocating engines, includes controls for blowers. Does not include units or components which are specifically included in other chapters. For general reports of engine control problems with insufficient information to file in a more specific JASC 7600 series code.
notes: Heuristic applicability; confirm against aircraft IPC/AMM/maintenance manual.
source: FAA Joint Aircraft System/Component Code Table and Definitions

title: Engine Synchronizing
definition: The components providing for engine synchronization in multi-engine aircraft.
notes: Heuristic applicability; confirm against aircraft IPC/AMM/maintenance manual.
source: FAA Joint Aircraft System/Component Code Table and Definitions

title: Mixture Control
definition: The control for adjusting fuel-air mixture in piston engines. Includes linkage from the cockpit lever to the carburetor or fuel injector servo but does not include the arm on mixture control shafts. Typical parts are cable, rod, bellcrank, rod end, housing, clamp and cockpit control lever/knob.
notes: Heuristic applicability; confirm against aircraft IPC/AMM/maintenance manual.
source: FAA Joint Aircraft System/Component Code Table and Definitions

title: Power Lever
definition: The system which provides for control of carburetor or fuel injectors on piston engines; fuel controls or coordinator on turbine engines; and propeller regulator turboprop engines. Typical parts are cable, rod, rod end, bellcrank, bracket, clamp, actuator, shaft, shaft pin, knob, etc.
notes: Heuristic applicability; confirm against aircraft IPC/AMM/maintenance manual.
source: FAA Joint Aircraft System/Component Code Table and Definitions

title: Engine Emergency Shutdown System
definition: The system which provides for rapid, complete shutoff of combustible fluids to the engine compartments during emergency procedures. Typical parts are cable, actuator, switch, lever, etc.
notes: Heuristic applicability; confirm against aircraft IPC/AMM/maintenance manual.
source: FAA Joint Aircraft System/Component Code Table and Definitions

title: Engine Control System Wiring
definition: For reports indicating a problem with wiring specific to the Engine Control System.
notes: Heuristic applicability; confirm against aircraft IPC/AMM/maintenance manual.
source: FAA Joint Aircraft System/Component Code Table and Definitions

title: Engine Indicating System
definition: For general reports of engine indicating system discrepancies with insufficient information to file in a more specific JASC 7700 series code. This code is also used for reports with multiple engine indications.
notes: Heuristic applicability; confirm against aircraft IPC/AMM/maintenance manual.
source: FAA Joint Aircraft System/Component Code Table and Definitions

title: Power Indicating System
definition: For power indicating systems which directly or indirectly indicates power or thrust (i.e., brake mean effective pressure {BMEP}, engine pressure ratio {EPR}, RPM, etc.) but is not covered in JASC codes 7711 through 7722.
notes: Heuristic applicability; confirm against aircraft IPC/AMM/maintenance manual.
source: FAA Joint Aircraft System/Component Code Table and Definitions

title: Engine Pressure Ratio (EPR)
definition: The system which sense, measures, and indicates the engine pressure ratio (EPR) of an turbine engine. The system measures the difference between the compressor inlet pressure and the turbine discharge pressure. Typical parts are sensor, transducer, transmitter, probe, etc.
notes: Heuristic applicability; confirm against aircraft IPC/AMM/maintenance manual.
source: FAA Joint Aircraft System/Component Code Table and Definitions

title: Engine BMEP/Torque Indicating
definition: The system that senses and measures brake mean effective pressure (BMEP) or engine torque in turbo- prop and piston engines. Does not include internal parts which are type certificated with the engine. Typical parts are indicator, line, sensor, transmitter, pressure switch, etc.
notes: Heuristic applicability; confirm against aircraft IPC/AMM/maintenance manual.
source: FAA Joint Aircraft System/Component Code Table and Definitions

title: Manifold Pressure (MP) Indicating
definition: The reciprocating engine manifold pressure (MP) indicating system including the indicator and sensor. Typical parts are lines, hoses and fittings.
notes: Heuristic applicability; confirm against aircraft IPC/AMM/maintenance manual.
source: FAA Joint Aircraft System/Component Code Table and Definitions

title: Engine RPM Indicating System
definition: The system including the indicator and sensor which indicates engine speed in revolutions per minute (RPM). Typical parts are, cable, connector, tachometer, tachometer generator, N1 indicator.
notes: Heuristic applicability; confirm against aircraft IPC/AMM/maintenance manual.
source: FAA Joint Aircraft System/Component Code Table and Definitions

title: Engine Temperature Indicating System
definition: For general reports of the system components and parts which indicate engine temperature with insufficient information to file in a more specific JASC 7700 series code.
notes: Heuristic applicability; confirm against aircraft IPC/AMM/maintenance manual.
source: FAA Joint Aircraft System/Component Code Table and Definitions

title: Cylinder Head Temperature (CHT) Indicating System
definition: The instruments which indicates temperature measured at reciprocating engine cylinder heads. Typical parts are indicator, case, dial, needle, thermocouple lead, sensor, and connector, etc.
notes: Heuristic applicability; confirm against aircraft IPC/AMM/maintenance manual.
source: FAA Joint Aircraft System/Component Code Table and Definitions

title: Engine EGT/TIT Indicating System
definition: For reports of exhaust gas temperature (EGT) or turbine inlet temperature (TIT) temperature sensing and indicating. Includes the EGT indicators for both reciprocating and turbine engines; and the TIT for turbine engines. Typical parts are wiring, turbine outlet temperature (TOT) indicator, EGT indicator, probe, harness, terminal, connector, indicator, sensor, transducer, transmitter, etc.
notes: Heuristic applicability; confirm against aircraft IPC/AMM/maintenance manual.
source: FAA Joint Aircraft System/Component Code Table and Definitions

title: Engine Ignition Analyzer System
definition: For general reports of reciprocating engine ignition analyzer system problems. Typical parts are the amplifier, wiring harness, and sensor, etc.
notes: Heuristic applicability; confirm against aircraft IPC/AMM/maintenance manual.
source: FAA Joint Aircraft System/Component Code Table and Definitions

title: Engine Ignition Analyzer
definition: The unit which interprets and indicates by oscilloscope the condition of ignition systems on reciprocating engines.
notes: Heuristic applicability; confirm against aircraft IPC/AMM/maintenance manual.
source: FAA Joint Aircraft System/Component Code Table and Definitions

title: Engine Vibration Analyzer
definition: For general reports of the engine vibration analyzer system indicating to the flight crew unusual engine vibration conditions. Typical parts are connector, harness, indicator, monitor, sensor, amplifier, etc.
notes: Heuristic applicability; confirm against aircraft IPC/AMM/maintenance manual.
source: FAA Joint Aircraft System/Component Code Table and Definitions

title: Engine Integrated Instrument System
definition: The portion of the system which is an integrated concept that receives engine operating parameters and transmits them to a central processor for cockpit presentation. Typical parts are the display units, transmitters, receivers, computers, etc.
notes: Heuristic applicability; confirm against aircraft IPC/AMM/maintenance manual.
source: FAA Joint Aircraft System/Component Code Table and Definitions

title: Engine Indicating System Wiring
definition: For reports indicating a problem with wiring specific to the Engine Indication System.
notes: Heuristic applicability; confirm against aircraft IPC/AMM/maintenance manual.
source: FAA Joint Aircraft System/Component Code Table and Definitions

title: Engine Exhaust System
definition: For general reports of engine exhaust system defects with insufficient information to file in a more specific JASC 7800 series code.
notes: Heuristic applicability; confirm against aircraft IPC/AMM/maintenance manual.
source: FAA Joint Aircraft System/Component Code Table and Definitions

title: Engine Collector/Tailpipe/Nozzle
definition: That portion of the system which collects the exhaust gases from the cylinders, turbines, or turbochargers and conducts them overboard. Includes variable vanes, or nacelle tailpipes used on turboprop powered aircraft and turbo-shaft powered rotorcraft. Typical parts are tailpipe, cone, nozzel, clamp eyebolt, duct, ejector, etc.
notes: Heuristic applicability; confirm against aircraft IPC/AMM/maintenance manual.
source: FAA Joint Aircraft System/Component Code Table and Definitions

title: Engine Noise Suppressor
definition: For general reports of muffler system defects. The component used on reciprocating engines to reduce engine exhaust noise. Does not include the shroud over the muffler used to collect heated fresh air for cabin and carburetor heat filed in JASC code 2140. Includes the clover leaf shaped unit mounted on turbo-jet engine exhaust tailpipes for sound suppression. Typical parts are baffle, and flame tube, etc.
notes: Heuristic applicability; confirm against aircraft IPC/AMM/maintenance manual.
source: FAA Joint Aircraft System/Component Code Table and Definitions

title: Engine Thrust Reverser
definition: The airframe furnished system and components mounted at turbo-jet engine exhaust tailpipes, or turbofan engine variable fan reverser components used to direct engine thrust forward for deceleration. Does not include the engine tailpipe. Typical parts are door, flex drive, relay, solenoid, switch, switch arm, bolt, valve, line, deploy line, rail, cable, actuator, actuator rod, connector plug, seal, support, fitting, shaft, link, nozzle, hose, etc.
notes: Heuristic applicability; confirm against aircraft IPC/AMM/maintenance manual.
source: FAA Joint Aircraft System/Component Code Table and Definitions

title: Engine Exhaust System Wiring
definition: For reports indicating a problem with wiring specific to the Engine Exhaust System
notes: Heuristic applicability; confirm against aircraft IPC/AMM/maintenance manual.
source: FAA Joint Aircraft System/Component Code Table and Definitions

title: Engine Oil System (Airframe Furnished)
definition: For general reports of system units external to the engine which store and deliver engine lubricating oil to and from both turbine and reciprocating engines with insufficient information to file in a more specific JASC 7900 series code.
notes: Heuristic applicability; confirm against aircraft IPC/AMM/maintenance manual.
source: FAA Joint Aircraft System/Component Code Table and Definitions

title: Engine Oil Storage (Airframe Furnished)
definition: The engine oil storage tank furnished by the airframe manufacturer. Includes attached parts such as filler caps, mount brackets, but excludes engine manufacturer furnished tanks, quantity indication systems, and distribution lines. Typical parts are tank, cap, seal, bracket, drain valve, etc.
notes: Heuristic applicability; confirm against aircraft IPC/AMM/maintenance manual.
source: FAA Joint Aircraft System/Component Code Table and Definitions

title: Engine Oil Distribution (Airframe Furnished)
definition: The external oil system which distributes engine lubricating oil from the storage tanks to and from the engine. Does not include externally mounted units such as oil coolers, oil filters, shutoff valves. Typical parts are line, hose, coupling, fitting, clamp, etc.
notes: Heuristic applicability; confirm against aircraft IPC/AMM/maintenance manual.
source: FAA Joint Aircraft System/Component Code Table and Definitions

title: Engine Oil Cooler
definition: The component and associated parts that cools engine lubricating oil. Includes brackets, outlet doors, scoops, ducts and louvers, but excludes the temperature regulator. Typical parts are cooler, duct, scoop, door, door actuator, etc.
notes: Heuristic applicability; confirm against aircraft IPC/AMM/maintenance manual.
source: FAA Joint Aircraft System/Component Code Table and Definitions

title: Engine Oil Temperature Regulator
definition: The unit which is mounted on the airframe oil cooler or the engine for controlling engine lubricating oil temperature. Typical parts are thermostat, thermal valve, regulator, etc.
notes: Heuristic applicability; confirm against aircraft IPC/AMM/maintenance manual.
source: FAA Joint Aircraft System/Component Code Table and Definitions

title: Engine Oil Shutoff Valve
definition: The component and associated controls which stop the flow of lubricating oil to the engine for emergency purposes.
notes: Heuristic applicability; confirm against aircraft IPC/AMM/maintenance manual.
source: FAA Joint Aircraft System/Component Code Table and Definitions

title: Engine Oil Indicating System
definition: For general reports of engine oil pressure, temperature and quantity and those reports with insufficient information to file in a more specific JASC 7900 series code. Includes oil filter bypass switch, chip detector light, indicators, etc.
notes: Heuristic applicability; confirm against aircraft IPC/AMM/maintenance manual.
source: FAA Joint Aircraft System/Component Code Table and Definitions

title: Engine Oil Pressure
definition: The instrument or warning lamp which indicates, senses, or transmits the pressure of engine lubricating oil available at the engine or when the pressure is improper for the conditions. This code is also used for discrepancies invloving oil pressure regulation. Typical parts are transducer, pressure switch, transmitter, pressure regulator, indicator, case, dial, needle, lamp, etc.
notes: Heuristic applicability; confirm against aircraft IPC/AMM/maintenance manual.
source: FAA Joint Aircraft System/Component Code Table and Definitions

title: Engine Oil Quantity
definition: The instrument or warning lamp which senses or indicates the quantity of oil in supply tanks or warns of an insufficient quantity. Typical parts are transmitter, indicator, case, lamp, etc.
notes: Heuristic applicability; confirm against aircraft IPC/AMM/maintenance manual.
source: FAA Joint Aircraft System/Component Code Table and Definitions

title: Engine Oil Temperature
definition: The instrument which senses and indicates temperature of engine oil. Typical parts are sensor, temperature bulb, case, indicator, needle, dial, etc.
notes: Heuristic applicability; confirm against aircraft IPC/AMM/maintenance manual.
source: FAA Joint Aircraft System/Component Code Table and Definitions

title: Engine Oil System Wiring
definition: For reports indicating a problem with wiring specific to the Engine Oil System.
notes: Heuristic applicability; confirm against aircraft IPC/AMM/maintenance manual.
source: FAA Joint Aircraft System/Component Code Table and Definitions

title: Engine Starting System
definition: The units, components and associated systems used for starting the engine. Includes electrical, inertia air or other starter systems. Does not include ignition systems which are covered in JASC Chapter 74, IGNITION.
notes: Heuristic applicability; confirm against aircraft IPC/AMM/maintenance manual.
source: FAA Joint Aircraft System/Component Code Table and Definitions

title: Engine Cranking
definition: The portion of the system which is used to perform the cranking functions of the starting operation. Typical parts are plumbing, valve, wiring, start switch, relay, etc.
notes: Heuristic applicability; confirm against aircraft IPC/AMM/maintenance manual.
source: FAA Joint Aircraft System/Component Code Table and Definitions

title: Engine Starter
definition: The component used for starting the engines. Includes parts which are separated from the engine during starter removals, but does not include parts within the engine. Does not include the starter- generator which is filed in JASC code 2435. Typical parts are brush, bearing, shaft, clutch, adaptor, backplate, housing, winding, terminal post, etc.
notes: Heuristic applicability; confirm against aircraft IPC/AMM/maintenance manual.
source: FAA Joint Aircraft System/Component Code Table and Definitions

title: Engine Start Valves/Controls
definition: The valves and controls used for starting engines.
notes: Heuristic applicability; confirm against aircraft IPC/AMM/maintenance manual.
source: FAA Joint Aircraft System/Component Code Table and Definitions

title: Engine Starting System Wiring
definition: For reports indicating a problem with wiring specific to the Engine Starting System.
notes: Heuristic applicability; confirm against aircraft IPC/AMM/maintenance manual.
source: FAA Joint Aircraft System/Component Code Table and Definitions

title: Exhaust Turbine System (Reciprocating)
definition: For reports of exhaust turbine systems for reciprocating engines. Includes power recovery turbine assemblies and turbocharger units when external to the engine.
notes: Heuristic applicability; confirm against aircraft IPC/AMM/maintenance manual.
source: FAA Joint Aircraft System/Component Code Table and Definitions

title: Power Recovery Turbine
definition: The turbines which extract energy from the exhaust gases and are coupled to the crankshaft on reciprocating engines. Includes the power recovery turbine unit when external to the engine. The drive shaft, coupling, and gears are filed in JASC code 8540.
notes: Heuristic applicability; confirm against aircraft IPC/AMM/maintenance manual.
source: FAA Joint Aircraft System/Component Code Table and Definitions

title: Exhaust Turbocharger
definition: For reports of airframe or engine manufacturer furnished exhaust driven turbocharger systems including the turbocharger unit, density controller and waste gate valve. Does not include the tailpipe. Typical parts are clamp, coupling, rod end, bracket, hose, scroll, bearing, impeller, shaft, etc.
notes: Heuristic applicability; confirm against aircraft IPC/AMM/maintenance manual.
source: FAA Joint Aircraft System/Component Code Table and Definitions

title: Turbocharger System Wiring
definition: For reports indicating a problem with wiring specific to the Turbocharger System.
notes: Heuristic applicability; confirm against aircraft IPC/AMM/maintenance manual.
source: FAA Joint Aircraft System/Component Code Table and Definitions

title: Water Injection System
definition: The system components and parts which inject a water mixture into induction system of turbine and reciprocating engines. Typical parts are pump, switch, tank, valve, etc.
notes: Heuristic applicability; confirm against aircraft IPC/AMM/maintenance manual.
source: FAA Joint Aircraft System/Component Code Table and Definitions

title: Water Injection System Wiring
definition: For reports indicating a problem with wiring specific to the Water Injkection System.
notes: Heuristic applicability; confirm against aircraft IPC/AMM/maintenance manual.
source: FAA Joint Aircraft System/Component Code Table and Definitions

title: Accessory Gearboxes
definition: The units and components which are remotely installed and connected to the engine by a drive shaft and which does not include those accessory drives which are bolted to and are immediately adjacent to the engine. The latter item should be filed in JASC code 7200. Does not include accessory drives bolted to and adjacent to engine which are normally filed in JASC code 7260.
notes: Heuristic applicability; confirm against aircraft IPC/AMM/maintenance manual.
source: FAA Joint Aircraft System/Component Code Table and Definitions

title: Accessory Gearbox System Wiring
definition: For reports indicating a problem with wiring specific to the Accessory Gearbox.
notes: Heuristic applicability; confirm against aircraft IPC/AMM/maintenance manual.
source: FAA Joint Aircraft System/Component Code Table and Definitions

title: Engine (Reciprocating)
definition: For general reports concerning reciprocating engine problems reported with insufficient information to file in a more specific JASC 8500 series code. Also for reports without reference to the applicable engine section or system. Typical reports would pertain to overtemperature, metal contamination, vibration, etc.
notes: Heuristic applicability; confirm against aircraft IPC/AMM/maintenance manual.
source: FAA Joint Aircraft System/Component Code Table and Definitions

title: Reciprocating Engine Front Section
definition: For reports of the piston engine front cases which contain the propeller shaft, reduction gears, and accessory drive. Typical parts are propeller shaft, gear, bearing, bushing, case, seal, pinion gear.
notes: Heuristic applicability; confirm against aircraft IPC/AMM/maintenance manual.
source: FAA Joint Aircraft System/Component Code Table and Definitions

title: Reciprocating Engine Power Section
definition: The section which contains the crankshaft, cam shaft, tappet guides, valve lifters, connecting rods, drive gears, etc. Does not include the push rods which are filed in the cylinder section in JASC code 8530 or rear case accessory drives. Typical parts are crankcase, crankshaft, cam ring, lifter, camshaft, cylinder stud, connecting rod, bolt, through bolt, cap, rod bolt, main bearing, rod bearing, etc.
notes: Heuristic applicability; confirm against aircraft IPC/AMM/maintenance manual.
source: FAA Joint Aircraft System/Component Code Table and Definitions

title: Reciprocating Engine Cylinder Section
definition: For reports of engine cylinders and associated parts including the intake pipes and valve push rods/housing. Also includes the cylinder baffles furnished by the engine manufacturer for engine cooling. Does not include the connecting rods or cylinder flange hold down bolts/studs which are in filed JASC code 8520. Typical parts are piston, piston pin, exhaust valve, intake valve, valve guide, rocker arm, valve cover, cylinder, pushrod housing, intake pipe, piston pin plug, valve spring, rocker shaft, piston ring, oil drain lines, clamp, baffles, etc.
notes: Heuristic applicability; confirm against aircraft IPC/AMM/maintenance manual.
source: FAA Joint Aircraft System/Component Code Table and Definitions

title: Reciprocating Engine Rear Section
definition: The case or section where accessories and associated engine drives are located. Includes the accessory pads, drives and drive seals but not the accessories. Does not include oil pump, filter or internal lubricating system which are filed in JASC code 8550. Typical parts are seal, gear, drive shaft, case, bearing, spacer. The power recovery turbine (PRT) drive shaft, coupling, and gears are also filed in this code.
notes: Heuristic applicability; confirm against aircraft IPC/AMM/maintenance manual.
source: FAA Joint Aircraft System/Component Code Table and Definitions

title: Reciprocating Engine Oil System
definition: The components and parts that provide oil pressure and distribute lubricating oil within the engine. Includes the plumbing leading to and from the using external systems and components which utilize engine system oil for operation. Does not include the externally mounted oil system storage tanks and connecting lines which are filed in JASC code 7910, or the oil cooler lines, hoses, and drain valves in JASC code 7920. Typical parts are pressure and scavenge pump, impeller, housing, filter, air-oil separator, crankcase breather, screen, element, relief valve, drive gear, adapter, pan, dipstick, cap, propeller governor oil lines, etc.
notes: Heuristic applicability; confirm against aircraft IPC/AMM/maintenance manual.
source: FAA Joint Aircraft System/Component Code Table and Definitions

title: Reciprocating Engine Supercharger
definition: The components and parts of the Supercharger system. Typical parts are case, impeller, rotors, bearings, seals, belts, pulleys or sprockets. Does not include gears in engine rear section.
notes: Heuristic applicability; confirm against aircraft IPC/AMM/maintenance manual.
source: FAA Joint Aircraft System/Component Code Table and Definitions

title: Reciprocating Engine Liquid Cooling
definition: The components and parts that provide cooling liquid to the engine. Includes the plumbing leading to and from the engine. Typical parts are radiator, hoses, pump, drive belt, pulleys, bearings, seals, overflow line, overflow tank, pressure cap, thermostat.
notes: Heuristic applicability; confirm against aircraft IPC/AMM/maintenance manual.
source: FAA Joint Aircraft System/Component Code Table and Definitions

title: Engine System Wiring
definition: For reports indicating a problem with wiring specific to the Reciprocating Engine System.
notes: Heuristic applicability; confirm against aircraft IPC/AMM/maintenance manual.
source: FAA Joint Aircraft System/Component Code Table and Definitions
